    93	def reassign_root_page_locale_on_delete(sender, instance, **kwargs):
    94	    # if we're deleting the locale used on the root page node, reassign that to a new locale first
    95	    root_page_with_this_locale = Page.objects.filter(depth=1, locale=instance)
    96	    if root_page_with_this_locale.exists():
    97	        # Select the default locale, if one exists and isn't the one being deleted
    98	        try:
    99	            new_locale = Locale.get_default()
   100	            default_locale_is_ok = new_locale != instance
   101	        except (Locale.DoesNotExist, LookupError):
   102	            default_locale_is_ok = False
   103	
   104	        if not default_locale_is_ok:
   105	            # fall back on any remaining locale
   106	            new_locale = Locale.all_objects.exclude(pk=instance.pk).first()
   107	
   108	        root_page_with_this_locale.update(locale=new_locale)

   144	class BasePageManager(MP_NodeManager):
   145	    def get_queryset(self):
   146	        return self._queryset_class(self.model).order_by("path")
   147	
   148	    def get_by_natural_key(self, url_path):
   149	        """Get page by URL"""
   150	        return self.get(url_path=url_path)
   151	
   152	    def first_common_ancestor_of(self, pages, include_self=False, strict=False):
   153	        """
   154	        This is similar to ``PageQuerySet.first_common_ancestor`` but works
   155	        for a list of pages instead of a queryset.
   156	        """
   157	        if not pages:
   158	            if strict:
   159	                raise self.model.DoesNotExist("Can not find ancestor of empty list")
   160	            return self.model.get_first_root_node()
   161	
   162	        if include_self:
   163	            paths = list({page.path for page in pages})
   164	        else:
   165	            paths = list({page.path[: -self.model.steplen] for page in pages})
   166	
   167	        # This method works on anything, not just file system paths.
   168	        common_parent_path = posixpath.commonprefix(paths)
   169	        extra_chars = len(common_parent_path) % self.model.steplen
   170	        if extra_chars != 0:
   171	            common_parent_path = common_parent_path[:-extra_chars]
   172	
   173	        if common_parent_path == "":
   174	            if strict:
   175	                raise self.model.DoesNotExist("No common ancestor found!")
   176	
   177	            return self.model.get_first_root_node()
   178	
   179	        return self.get(path=common_parent_path)

   181	    def annotate_parent_page(self, pages):
   182	        """
   183	        Annotates each page with its parent page. This is implemented as a
   184	        manager-only method instead of a QuerySet method so it can be used with
   185	        search results.
   186	
   187	        If given a QuerySet, this method will evaluate it. Only use this method
   188	        when you are ready to consume the queryset, e.g. after pagination has
   189	        been applied. This is typically done in the view's ``get_context_data``
   190	        using ``context["object_list"]``.
   191	
   192	        This method does not return a new queryset, but modifies the existing one,
   193	        to ensure any references to the queryset in the view's context are updated
   194	        (e.g. when using ``context_object_name``).
   195	        """
   196	        parent_page_paths = {
   197	            Page._get_parent_path_from_path(page.path) for page in pages
   198	        }
   199	        parent_pages_by_path = {
   200	            page.path: page
   201	            for page in Page.objects.filter(path__in=parent_page_paths).specific(
   202	                defer=True
   203	            )
   204	        }
   205	        for page in pages:
   206	            parent_page = parent_pages_by_path.get(
   207	                Page._get_parent_path_from_path(page.path)
   208	            )
   209	            page._parent_page = parent_page

   498	    @staticmethod
   499	    def route_for_request(request: HttpRequest, path: str) -> RouteResult | None:
   500	        """
   501	        Find the page route for the given HTTP request object, and URL path. The route
   502	        result (`page`, `args`, and `kwargs`) will be cached via
   503	        ``request._wagtail_route_for_request``.
   504	        """
   505	        if not hasattr(request, "_wagtail_route_for_request"):
   506	            try:
   507	                # we need a valid Site object for this request in order to proceed
   508	                if site := Site.find_for_request(request):
   509	                    path_components = [
   510	                        component for component in path.split("/") if component
   511	                    ]
   512	                    request._wagtail_route_for_request = (
   513	                        site.root_page.localized.specific.route(
   514	                            request, path_components
   515	                        )
   516	                    )
   517	                else:
   518	                    request._wagtail_route_for_request = None
   519	            except Http404:
   520	                # .route() can raise Http404
   521	                request._wagtail_route_for_request = None
   522	
   523	        return request._wagtail_route_for_request

   529	        page route will be cached via ``request._wagtail_route_for_request``.
   530	        """
   531	        result = Page.route_for_request(request, path)
   532	        if result is not None:
   533	            return result[0]

   577	    def set_url_path(self, parent):
   578	        """
   579	        Populate the url_path field based on this page's slug and the specified parent page.
   580	        (We pass a parent in here, rather than retrieving it via get_parent, so that we can give
   581	        new unsaved pages a meaningful URL when previewing them; at that point the page has not
   582	        been assigned a position in the tree, as far as treebeard is concerned.
   583	        """

   585	            self.url_path = parent.url_path + self.slug + "/"
   586	        else:
   587	            # a page without a parent is the tree root, which always has a url_path of '/'
   588	            self.url_path = "/"

   592	    @staticmethod
   593	    def _slug_is_available(slug, parent_page, page=None):
   594	        """
   595	        Determine whether the given slug is available for use on a child page of
   596	        parent_page. If 'page' is passed, the slug is intended for use on that page
   597	        (and so it will be excluded from the duplicate check).
   598	        """
   599	        if parent_page is None:
   600	            # the root page's slug can be whatever it likes...
   601	            return True
   602	
   603	        siblings = parent_page.get_children()
   604	        if page:
   605	            siblings = siblings.not_page(page)
   606	
   607	        return not siblings.filter(slug=slug).exists()

   609	    def _get_autogenerated_slug(self, base_slug):
   610	        candidate_slug = base_slug
   611	        suffix = 1
   612	        parent_page = self.get_parent()
   613	
   614	        while not Page._slug_is_available(candidate_slug, parent_page, self):
   615	            # try with incrementing suffix until we find a slug which is available
   616	            suffix += 1
   617	            candidate_slug = "%s-%d" % (base_slug, suffix)
   618	
   619	        return candidate_slug

   645	    def _set_core_field_defaults(self):

   652	        # final save, and there is no point trying to auto-generate a slug yet, as we
   653	        # don't know the set of sibling pages to de-duplicate against. In this case,
   654	        # this method will be called again as part of the call to `save()` after the
   655	        # path is populated, and we will auto-generate the slug at that point if
   656	        # necessary.

   662	            # only proceed if we get a non-empty base slug back from slugify

   677	    def _check_slug_is_unique(self):

   713	    @transaction.atomic
   714	    # ensure that changes are only committed when we have updated all descendant URL paths, to preserve consistency
   715	    def save(self, clean=True, user=None, log_action=False, **kwargs):
   716	        """
   717	        Writes the page to the database, performing additional housekeeping tasks to ensure data
   718	        integrity:
   719	
   720	        * ``locale``, ``draft_title`` and ``slug`` are set to default values if not provided, with ``slug``
   721	          being generated from the title with a suffix to ensure uniqueness within the parent page
   722	          where necessary
   723	        * The ``url_path`` field is set based on the ``slug`` and the parent page
   724	        * If the ``slug`` has changed, the ``url_path`` of this page and all descendants is updated and
   725	          a :ref:`page_slug_changed` signal is sent
   726	
   727	        New pages should be saved by passing the unsaved page instance to the
   728	        :meth:`~treebeard.mp_tree.MP_Node.add_child`
   729	        or :meth:`~treebeard.mp_tree.MP_Node.add_sibling` method of an existing page, which will correctly update
   730	        the fields responsible for tracking the page's location in the tree.
   731	
   732	        If ``clean=False`` is passed, the page is saved without validation. This is appropriate for updates that only
   733	        change metadata such as `latest_revision` while keeping content and page location unchanged.
   734	
   735	        If ``clean=True`` is passed (the default), and the page has ``live=True`` set, the page is validated using
   736	        :meth:`~django.db.models.Model.full_clean` before saving.
   737	
   738	        If ``clean=True`` is passed, and the page has ``live=False`` set, only the title and slug fields are validated.
   739	        """
   740	        if clean:
   741	            if self.live:
   742	                self.full_clean()
   743	            else:
   744	                # Saving as draft; only perform the minimal validation to satisfy data integrity
   745	                self.minimal_clean()
   746	
   747	        slug_changed = False
   748	        is_new = self.id is None
   749	
   750	        if is_new:
   751	            # we are creating a record. If we're doing things properly, this should happen
   752	            # through a treebeard method like add_child, in which case the 'path' field
   753	            # has been set and so we can safely call get_parent
   754	            self.set_url_path(self.get_parent())
   755	        else:
   756	            # Check that we are committing the slug to the database
   757	            # Basically: If update_fields has been specified, and slug is not included, skip this step
   758	            if not (
   759	                "update_fields" in kwargs
   760	                and kwargs["update_fields"] is not None
   761	                and "slug" not in kwargs["update_fields"]
   762	            ):
   763	                # see if the slug has changed from the record in the db, in which case we need to
   764	                # update url_path of self and all descendants. Even though we might not need it,
   765	                # the specific page is fetched here for sending to the 'page_slug_changed' signal.
   766	                old_record = Page.objects.get(id=self.id).specific
   767	                if old_record.slug != self.slug:
   768	                    self.set_url_path(self.get_parent())
   769	                    slug_changed = True
   770	                    old_url_path = old_record.url_path
   771	                    new_url_path = self.url_path
   772	
   773	        result = super().save(**kwargs)
   774	
   775	        if slug_changed:
   776	            self._update_descendant_url_paths(old_url_path, new_url_path)
   777	            # Emit page_slug_changed signal on successful db commit
   778	            transaction.on_commit(
   779	                lambda: page_slug_changed.send(
   780	                    sender=self.specific_class or self.__class__,
   781	                    instance=self.specific,
   782	                    instance_before=old_record,
   783	                )
   784	            )
   785	
   786	        # Check if this is a root page of any sites and clear the 'wagtail_site_root_paths' key if so
   787	        # Note: New translations of existing site roots are considered site roots as well, so we must
   788	        # always check if this page is a site root, even if it's new.
   789	        if self.is_site_root():
   790	            Site.clear_site_root_paths_cache()

   792	        # Log
   793	        if is_new:
   794	            cls = type(self)
   795	            logger.info(
   796	                'Page created: "%s" id=%d content_type=%s.%s path=%s',
   797	                self.title,
   798	                self.id,
   799	                cls._meta.app_label,
   800	                cls.__name__,
   801	                self.url_path,
   802	            )
   803	
   804	        if log_action is not None:
   805	            # The default for log_action is False. i.e. don't log unless specifically instructed
   806	            # Page creation is a special case that we want logged by default, but allow skipping it
   807	            # explicitly by passing log_action=None
   808	            if is_new:
   809	                log(
   810	                    instance=self,
   811	                    action="wagtail.create",
   812	                    user=user or self.owner,
   813	                    content_changed=True,
   814	                )
   815	            elif log_action:
   816	                log(instance=self, action=log_action, user=user)
   817	
   818	        return result

   824	    @classmethod
   825	    def check(cls, **kwargs):
   826	        errors = super().check(**kwargs)
   827	
   828	        # Check that foreign keys from pages are not configured to cascade
   829	        # This is the default Django behaviour which must be explicitly overridden
   830	        # to prevent pages disappearing unexpectedly and the tree being corrupted
   831	
   832	        # get names of foreign keys pointing to parent classes (such as page_ptr)
   833	        field_exceptions = [
   834	            field.name
   835	            for model in [cls] + list(cls._meta.get_parent_list())
   836	            for field in model._meta.parents.values()
   837	            if field
   838	        ]
   839	
   840	        for field in cls._meta.fields:
   841	            if (
   842	                isinstance(field, models.ForeignKey)
   843	                and field.name not in field_exceptions
   844	            ):
   845	                if field.remote_field.on_delete == models.CASCADE:
   846	                    errors.append(
   847	                        checks.Warning(
   848	                            "Field hasn't specified on_delete action",
   849	                            hint="Set on_delete=models.SET_NULL and make sure the field is nullable or set on_delete=models.PROTECT. Wagtail does not allow simple database CASCADE because it will corrupt its tree storage.",
   850	                            obj=field,
   851	                            id="wagtailcore.W001",
   852	                        )
   853	                    )
   854	
   855	        if not isinstance(cls.objects, PageManager):
   856	            errors.append(
   857	                checks.Error(
   858	                    "Manager does not inherit from PageManager",
   859	                    hint="Ensure that custom Page managers inherit from wagtail.models.PageManager",
   860	                    obj=cls,
   861	                    id="wagtailcore.E002",
   862	                )
   863	            )
   864	
   865	        try:
   866	            cls.clean_subpage_models()
   867	        except (ValueError, LookupError) as e:
   868	            errors.append(
   869	                checks.Error(
   870	                    "Invalid subpage_types setting for %s" % cls,
   871	                    hint=str(e),
   872	                    id="wagtailcore.E002",
   873	                )
   874	            )
   875	
   876	        try:
   877	            cls.clean_parent_page_models()
   878	        except (ValueError, LookupError) as e:
   879	            errors.append(
   880	                checks.Error(
   881	                    "Invalid parent_page_types setting for %s" % cls,
   882	                    hint=str(e),
   883	                    id="wagtailcore.E002",
   884	                )
   885	            )
   886	
   887	        return errors

   889	    def _update_descendant_url_paths(self, old_url_path, new_url_path):
   890	        (
   891	            Page.objects.filter(path__startswith=self.path)
   892	            .exclude(pk=self.pk)

   895	                    Value(new_url_path), Substr("url_path", len(old_url_path) + 1)
   896	                )
   897	            )

The direct-reference search is broad enough that it includes generic mixins and tests, so I’m splitting it by package directory and reading the production files that actually call `Page`, `Page.objects`, `specific`, `url_path`, `alias_of`, locale/translation fields, or page-related foreign keys. This should give a usable flat list instead of a noisy grep dump.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc "rg -n \"\\bPage\\b|Page\\.objects|page_ptr|url_path|alias_of|latest_revision|live_revision|translation_key|locale_id|get_page_models|WorkflowPage|GroupPagePermission|PageViewRestriction|PageSubscription\" wagtail/models wagtail/permission_policies wagtail/management wagtail/rich_text wagtail/search wagtail/views.py wagtail/signal_handlers.py wagtail/urls.py wagtail/workflows.py wagtail/locks.py wagtail/permissions.py -g '*.py' -g '"'!**/tests/**'"' -g '"'!**/migrations/**'"'"

   182	    def select_related(self, *fields, for_specific_subqueries: bool = False):

   189	        exactly like the original method. However, when ``True``, ``fields`` are
   190	        **required**, and must match names of ForeignKey fields on all specific models
   191	        that might be included in the result (which can include fields inherited from
   192	        concrete parents). Unlike when ``for_specific_subqueries`` is ``False``, no
   193	        validation is applied to ``fields`` when the method is called. Rather, that when
   194	        the method is called. Instead, that validation is applied for each individual
   195	        subquery when the queryset is evaluated. This difference in behaviour should be
   196	        taken into account when experimenting with ``for_specific_subqueries=True`` .
   197	
   198	        As with Django's native implementation, you chain multiple applications of
   199	        ``select_related()`` with ``for_specific_subqueries=True`` to progressively add
   200	        to the list of fields to be fetched. For example:
   201	
   202	        .. code-block:: python
   203	
   204	            # Fetch 'author' when retrieving specific page data
   205	            queryset = Page.objects.specific().select_related("author", for_specific_subqueries=True)
   206	
   207	            # We're rendering cards with images, so fetch the listing image too
   208	            queryset = queryset.select_related("listing_image", for_specific_subqueries=True)
   209

   211	            queryset = queryset.select_related("topic", "target_audience", for_specific_subqueries=True)
   212	
   213	        As with Django's native implementation, ``None`` can be supplied in place of
   214	        ``fields`` to negate a previous application of ``select_related()``. By default,
   215	        this will only work for cases where ``select_related()`` was called without
   216	        ``for_specific_subqueries``, or with ``for_specific_subqueries=False``. However,
   217	        you can use ``for_specific_subqueries=True`` to negate subquery-specific

   220	        .. code-block:: python

   223	            queryset = Page.objects.specific().select_related(
   224	                "author",
   225	                "listing_image",
   226	                for_specific_subqueries=True
   227	            )
   228	
   229	            # I've changed my mind. Do not fetch any additional data
   230	            queryset = queryset.select_related(None, for_specific_subqueries=True)

   429	    def first_common_ancestor(self, include_self=False, strict=False):
   430	        """
   431	        Find the first ancestor that all pages in this queryset have in common.
   432	        For example, consider a page hierarchy like::
   433	
   434	            - Home/
   435	                - Foo Event Index/
   436	                    - Foo Event Page 1/
   437	                    - Foo Event Page 2/
   438	                - Bar Event Index/
   439	                    - Bar Event Page 1/
   440	                    - Bar Event Page 2/
   441	
   442	        The common ancestors for some queries would be:
   443	
   444	        .. code-block:: python
   445	
   446	            >>> Page.objects\\
   447	            ...     .type(EventPage)\\
   448	            ...     .first_common_ancestor()
   449	            <Page: Home>
   450	            >>> Page.objects\\
   451	            ...     .type(EventPage)\\
   452	            ...     .filter(title__contains='Foo')\\
   453	            ...     .first_common_ancestor()
   454	            <Page: Foo Event Index>
   455	
   456	        This method tries to be efficient, but if you have millions of pages
   457	        scattered across your page tree, it will be slow.
   458	
   459	        If `include_self` is True, the ancestor can be one of the pages in the
   460	        queryset:
   461	
   462	        .. code-block:: python
   463	
   464	            >>> Page.objects\\
   465	            ...     .filter(title__contains='Foo')\\
   466	            ...     .first_common_ancestor()
   467	            <Page: Foo Event Index>
   468	            >>> Page.objects\\
   469	            ...     .filter(title__exact='Bar Event Index')\\
   470	            ...     .first_common_ancestor()
   471	            <Page: Bar Event Index>
   472	
   473	        A few invalid cases exist: when the queryset is empty, when the root
   474	        Page is in the queryset and ``include_self`` is False, and when there
   475	        are multiple page trees with no common root (a case Wagtail does not
   476	        support). If ``strict`` is False (the default), then the first root
   477	        node is returned in these cases. If ``strict`` is True, then a
   478	        ``ObjectDoesNotExist`` is raised.
   479	        """
   480	        # An empty queryset has no ancestors. This is a problem
   481	        if not self.exists():
   482	            if strict:
   483	                raise self.model.DoesNotExist("Can not find ancestor of empty queryset")
   484	            return self.model.get_first_root_node()
   485	
   486	        if include_self:
   487	            # Get all the paths of the matched pages.
   488	            paths = self.order_by().values_list("path", flat=True)
   489	        else:
   490	            # Find all the distinct parent paths of all matched pages.
   491	            # The empty `.order_by()` ensures that `Page.path` is not also
   492	            # selected to order the results, which makes `.distinct()` works.
   493	            paths = (
   494	                self.order_by()
   495	                .annotate(
   496	                    parent_path=Substr(
   497	                        "path",
   498	                        1,
   499	                        Length("path") - self.model.steplen,
   500	                        output_field=CharField(max_length=255),
   501	                    )
   502	                )
   503	                .values_list("parent_path", flat=True)
   504	                .distinct()
   505	            )
   506	
   507	        # This method works on anything, not just file system paths.
   508	        common_parent_path = posixpath.commonprefix(paths)
   509	
   510	        # That may have returned a path like (0001, 0002, 000), which is
   511	        # missing some chars off the end. Fix this by trimming the path to a
   512	        # multiple of `Page.steplen`
   513	        extra_chars = len(common_parent_path) % self.model.steplen
   514	        if extra_chars != 0:
   515	            common_parent_path = common_parent_path[:-extra_chars]
   516	
   517	        if common_parent_path == "":
   518	            # This should only happen when there are multiple trees,
   519	            # a situation that Wagtail does not support;
   520	            # or when the root node itself is part of the queryset.

   221	        first - used to translate url_paths into actual URLs with hostnames.
   222	
   223	        Each root path is an instance of the `SiteRootPath` named tuple,
   224	        and have the following attributes:

   228	        - ``root_url`` - The scheme/domain name of the site (for example 'https://www.example.com/')
   229	        - ``language_code`` - The language code of the site (for example 'en')
   230	        """
   231	        result = cache.get(
   232	            SITE_ROOT_PATHS_CACHE_KEY, version=SITE_ROOT_PATHS_CACHE_VERSION
   233	        )
   234	
   235	        if result is None:
   236	            result = []
   237	
   238	            for site in Site.objects.select_related(
   239	                "root_page", "root_page__locale"
   240	            ).order_by("-root_page__url_path", "-is_default_site", "hostname"):

   247	                                site.root_url,
   248	                                root_page.locale.language_code,
   249	                            )
   250	                            for root_page in site.root_page.get_translations(
   251	                                inclusive=True
   252	                            ).select_related("locale")
   253	                        ]
   254	                    )
   255	                else:
   256	                    result.append(
   257	                        SiteRootPath(
   258	                            site.id,
   259	                            site.root_page.url_path,
   260	                            site.root_url,
   261	                            site.root_page.locale.language_code,
   262	                        )
   263	                    )
   264	
   265	            cache.set(
   266	                SITE_ROOT_PATHS_CACHE_KEY,
   267	                result,
   268	                3600,
   269	                version=SITE_ROOT_PATHS_CACHE_VERSION,
   270	            )
   271	
   272	        else:
   273	            # Convert the cache result to a list of SiteRootPath tuples, as some
   274	            # cache backends (e.g. Redis) don't support named tuples.
   275	            result = [SiteRootPath(*result) for result in result]
   276	
   277	        return result

  1098	    def update_aliases(self, *, revision=None, _content=None, _updated_ids=None):

  1100	        Publishes all aliases that follow this page with the latest content from this page.
  1101	
  1102	        This is called by Wagtail whenever a page with aliases is published.
  1103	
  1104	        :param revision: The revision of the original page that we are updating to (used for logging purposes)
  1105	        :type revision: Revision, Optional
  1106	        """
  1107	        specific_self = self.specific
  1108	
  1109	        # Only compute this if necessary since it's quite a heavy operation
  1110	        if _content is None:
  1111	            _content = self.serializable_data()
  1112

  1114	        # created an alias loop (which is impossible to do with the UI Wagtail provides)
  1115	        _updated_ids = _updated_ids or []

  1117	        for alias in self.specific_class.objects.filter(alias_of=self).exclude(
  1118	            id__in=_updated_ids
  1119	        ):
  1120	            # FIXME: Switch to the same fields that are excluded from copy
  1121	            # We can't do this right now because we can't exclude fields from with_content_json
  1122	            exclude_fields = [
  1123	                "id",
  1124	                "path",
  1125	                "depth",
  1126	                "numchild",
  1127	                "url_path",
  1128	                "path",
  1129	                "index_entries",
  1130	                "postgres_index_entries",
  1131	            ]

  1133	            # Copy field content

  1135	
  1136	            # Publish the alias if it's currently in draft

  1138	            alias_updated.has_unpublished_changes = False
  1139	
  1140	            # Copy child relations
  1141	            child_object_map = specific_self.copy_all_child_relations(
  1142	                target=alias_updated, exclude=exclude_fields
  1143	            )
  1144	
  1145	            # Process child objects
  1146	            # This has two jobs:
  1147	            #  - If the alias is in a different locale, this updates the
  1148	            #    locale of any translatable child objects to match
  1149	            #  - If the alias is not a translation of the original, this
  1150	            #    changes the translation_key field of all child objects
  1151	            #    so they do not clash

  1153	                alias_is_translation = alias.translation_key == self.translation_key

  1155	                def process_child_object(child_object):

  1166	                for (rel, previous_id), child_objects in child_object_map.items():
  1167	                    if previous_id is None:
  1168	                        for child_object in child_objects:
  1169	                            process_child_object(child_object)
  1170	                    else:
  1171	                        process_child_object(child_objects)

  1173	            # Copy M2M relations

  1175	                specific_self, alias_updated, exclude_fields=exclude_fields
  1176	            )

  1180	            alias_updated.slug = alias.slug

  1190	                sender=alias_updated.specific_class,
  1191	                instance=alias_updated,
  1192	                revision=revision,
  1193	                alias=True,
  1194	            )
  1195	
  1196	            # Update any aliases of that alias
  1197	
  1198	            # Design note:
  1199	            # It could be argued that this will be faster if we just changed these alias-of-alias
  1200	            # pages to all point to the original page and avoid having to update them recursively.
  1201	            #
  1202	            # But, it's useful to have a record of how aliases have been chained.
  1203	            # For example, In Wagtail Localize, we use aliases to create mirrored trees, but those
  1204	            # trees themselves could have aliases within them. If an alias within a tree is
  1205	            # converted to a regular page, we want the alias in the mirrored tree to follow that
  1206	            # new page and stop receiving updates from the original page.
  1207	            #

  1209	            # comparison to the work required to update the alias.

  1211	            alias.update_aliases(
  1212	                revision=revision,
  1213	                _content=_content,
  1214	                _updated_ids=_updated_ids,
  1215	            )

  1280	    def check_request_method(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
  1281	        """
  1282	        Checks the ``method`` attribute of the request against those supported
  1283	        by the page (as defined by :attr:`allowed_http_methods`) and responds
  1284	        accordingly.
  1285	
  1286	        If supported, ``None`` is returned, and the request is processed
  1287	        normally. If not, a warning is logged and an ``HttpResponseNotAllowed``
  1288	        is returned, and any further request handling is terminated.
  1289	        """
  1290	        allowed_methods = self.allowed_http_method_names()
  1291	        if request.method not in allowed_methods:
  1292	            response = HttpResponseNotAllowed(allowed_methods)
  1293	            log_response(
  1294	                "Method Not Allowed (%s): %s",
  1295	                request.method,
  1296	                request.path,
  1297	                request=request,
  1298	                response=response,
  1299	            )
  1300	            return response

  1302	    def handle_options_request(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
  1303	        """
  1304	        Returns an ``HttpResponse`` with an ``"Allow"`` header containing the list of
  1305	        supported HTTP methods for this page. This method is used instead of
  1306	        :meth:`serve` to handle requests when the ``OPTIONS`` HTTP verb is
  1307	        detected (and :class:`HTTPMethod.OPTIONS <python:http.HTTPMethod>` is
  1308	        present in :attr:`allowed_http_methods` for this type of page).
  1309	        """
  1310	        return HttpResponse(
  1311	            headers={"Allow": ", ".join(self.allowed_http_method_names())}
  1312	        )

  1322	    def _get_site_root_paths(self, cache_object=None, **kwargs):

  1330	                category=RemovedInWagtail90Warning,
  1331	            )
  1332	            cache_object = cache_object or kwargs["request"]
  1333	        # if we have a cache_object, use that to cache site_root_paths; otherwise, use self
  1334	        cache_object = cache_object if cache_object else self

  1336	            return cache_object._wagtail_cached_site_root_paths
  1337	        except AttributeError:
  1338	            cache_object._wagtail_cached_site_root_paths = Site.get_site_root_paths()
  1339	            return cache_object._wagtail_cached_site_root_paths

  1341	    def _get_relevant_site_root_paths(self, cache_object=None):

  1346	            srp

  1348	            if self.url_path.startswith(srp.root_path)

  1351	    def get_url_parts(self, request=None):

  1359	        and ``get_site`` properties and methods; pages with custom URL routing

  1361	        the custom URLs.

  1364	        to avoid repeated database / cache lookups. Typically, a page model

  1376	        # the first item is ideal in the vast majority of setups.

  1383	            # site and prefer an option matching that (where present).

  1431	    def get_full_url(self, request=None):

  1447	    def get_url(self, request=None, current_site=None):
  1448	        """
  1449	        Return the 'most appropriate' URL for referring to this page from the pages we serve,
  1450	        within the Wagtail backend and actual website templates;
  1451	        this is the local URL (starting with '/') if we're only running a single site
  1452	        (i.e. we know that whatever the current page is being served from, this link will be on the
  1453	        same domain), and the full URL (with domain) if not.
  1454	        Return ``None`` if the page is not routable.
  1455	
  1456	        Accepts an optional but recommended ``request`` keyword argument that, if provided, will
  1457	        be used to cache site-level URL information (thereby avoiding repeated database / cache
  1458	        lookups) and, via the ``Site.find_for_request()`` function, determine whether a relative
  1459	        or full URL is most appropriate.
  1460	        """
  1461	        # ``current_site`` is purposefully undocumented, as one can simply pass the request and get
  1462	        # a relative URL based on ``Site.find_for_request()``. Nonetheless, support it here to avoid
  1463	        # copy/pasting the code to the ``relative_url`` method below.
  1464	        if current_site is None and request is not None:
  1465	            site = Site.find_for_request(request)
  1466	            current_site = site
  1467	        url_parts = self.get_url_parts(request=request)
  1468	
  1469	        if url_parts is None or url_parts[1] is None and url_parts[2] is None:
  1470	            # page is not routable
  1471	            return
  1472	
  1473	        site_id, root_url, page_path = url_parts
  1474	
  1475	        # Get number of unique sites in root paths
  1476	        # Note: there may be more root paths to sites if there are multiple languages
  1477	        num_sites = len(
  1478	            {root_path[0] for root_path in self._get_site_root_paths(request)}
  1479	        )
  1480	
  1481	        if (current_site is not None and site_id == current_site.id) or num_sites == 1:
  1482	            # the site matches OR we're only running a single site, so a local URL is sufficient
  1483	            return page_path
  1484	        else:
  1485	            return root_url + page_path

  1489	    def relative_url(self, current_site, request=None):

  1491	        Return the 'most appropriate' URL for this page taking into account the site we're currently on;
  1492	        a local URL if the site matches, or a fully qualified one otherwise.

  1496	        be used to cache site-level URL information (thereby avoiding repeated database / cache
  1497	        lookups).

  1535	    @classmethod
  1536	    def clean_subpage_models(cls):

  1540	        or LookupError if a model does not exist (or is not a Page subclass).

  1544	            if subpage_types is None:
  1545	                # if subpage_types is not specified on the Page class, allow all page types as subpages
  1546	                cls._clean_subpage_models = get_page_models()
  1547	            else:
  1548	                cls._clean_subpage_models = [
  1549	                    resolve_model_string(model_string, cls._meta.app_label)
  1550	                    for model_string in subpage_types
  1551	                ]
  1552	
  1553	                for model in cls._clean_subpage_models:
  1554	                    if not issubclass(model, Page):
  1555	                        raise LookupError("%s is not a Page subclass" % model)

  1557	        return cls._clean_subpage_models

  1571	                cls._clean_parent_page_models = get_page_models()

  1584	    @classmethod

  1588	        as a list of model classes.

  1602	        return [

  1620	    @classmethod
  1621	    def can_exist_under(cls, parent):

  1623	        Checks if this page type can exist as a subpage under a parent page
  1624	        instance.
  1625	
  1626	        See also: :func:`Page.can_create_at` and :func:`Page.can_move_to`

  1630	    @classmethod
  1631	    def can_create_at(cls, parent):
  1632	        """
  1633	        Checks if this page type can be created as a subpage under a parent
  1634	        page instance.
  1635	        """
  1636	        can_create = cls.is_creatable and cls.can_exist_under(parent)
  1637	
  1638	        if cls.max_count is not None:
  1639	            can_create = can_create and cls.objects.count() < cls.max_count
  1640	
  1641	        if cls.max_count_per_parent is not None:
  1642	            can_create = (
  1643	                can_create
  1644	                and parent.get_children().type(cls).count() < cls.max_count_per_parent
  1645	            )
  1646	
  1647	        return can_create

  1649	    def can_move_to(self, parent):
  1650	        """
  1651	        Checks if this page instance can be moved to be a subpage of a parent
  1652	        page instance.
  1653	        """
  1654	        # Prevent pages from being moved to different language sections
  1655	        # The only page that can have multi-lingual children is the root page
  1656	        parent_is_root = parent.depth == 1
  1657	        if not parent_is_root and parent.locale_id != self.locale_id:
  1658	            return False
  1659	
  1660	        # Must be able to exist under parent
  1661	        if not self.can_exist_under(parent):
  1662	            return False
  1663	
  1664	        # If the page has a max_count_per_parent, check for a page of this type under the destination
  1665	        if self.max_count_per_parent is not None:
  1666	            return (
  1667	                parent.get_children().type(self.specific_class).not_page(self).count()
  1668	                < self.max_count_per_parent
  1669	            )
  1670	
  1671	        return True

  1818	    def is_previewable(self):

  1821	        # if so, checking self.preview_modes would incorrectly give us the default set from
  1822	        # Page.preview_modes. However, accessing self.specific.preview_modes would result in an N+1
  1823	        # query problem. To avoid this (at least in the general case), we'll call .specific only if
  1824	        # a check of the property at the class level indicates that preview_modes has been
  1825	        # overridden from whatever type we're currently in.
  1826	        page = self
  1827	        if page.specific_class.preview_modes != type(page).preview_modes:

  1870	    @property
  1871	    def cache_key(self):

  1873	        A generic cache key to identify a page in its current state.
  1874	        Should the page change, so will the key.
  1875

  1878

  1925	    def get_view_restrictions(self):
  1926	        """
  1927	        Return a query set of all page view restrictions that apply to this page.
  1928	
  1929	        This checks the current page and all ancestor pages for page view restrictions.
  1930	
  1931	        If any of those pages are aliases, it will resolve them to their source pages
  1932	        before querying PageViewRestrictions so alias pages use the same view restrictions
  1933	        as their source page and they cannot have their own.
  1934	        """
  1935	        page_ids_to_check = set()
  1936	
  1937	        def add_page_to_check_list(page):
  1938	            # If the page is an alias, add the source page to the check list instead
  1939	            if page.alias_of:
  1940	                add_page_to_check_list(page.alias_of)
  1941	            else:
  1942	                page_ids_to_check.add(page.id)
  1943	
  1944	        # Check current page for view restrictions
  1945	        add_page_to_check_list(self)
  1946	
  1947	        # Check each ancestor for view restrictions as well
  1948	        for page in self.get_ancestors().only("alias_of"):
  1949	            add_page_to_check_list(page)
  1950	
  1951	        return PageViewRestriction.objects.filter(page_id__in=page_ids_to_check)

  1955	    def serve_password_required_response(self, request, form, action_url):
  1956	        """
  1957	        Serve a response indicating that the user has been denied access to view this page,
  1958	        and must supply a password.
  1959	        ``form`` = a Django form object containing the password input
  1960	            (and zero or more hidden fields that also need to be output on the template)
  1961	        ``action_url`` = URL that this form should be POSTed to
  1962	        """
  1963	
  1964	        password_required_template = self.password_required_template or getattr(
  1965	            settings,
  1966	            "WAGTAIL_PASSWORD_REQUIRED_TEMPLATE",
  1967	            "wagtailcore/password_required.html",
  1968	        )
  1969	
  1970	        # Ensuring preview attributes exist
  1971	        if not hasattr(request, "is_preview"):
  1972	            request.is_preview = False
  1973	        if not hasattr(request, "preview_mode"):
  1974	            request.preview_mode = None
  1975	
  1976	        context = self.get_context(request)
  1977	        context["form"] = form
  1978	        context["action_url"] = action_url
  1979	        return TemplateResponse(request, password_required_template, context)

  1981	    def with_content_json(self, content):
  1982	        """
  1983	        Returns a new version of the page with field values updated to reflect changes
  1984	        in the provided ``content`` (which usually comes from a previously-saved
  1985	        page revision).
  1986	
  1987	        Certain field values are preserved in order to prevent errors if the returned
  1988	        page is saved, such as ``id``, ``content_type`` and some tree-related values.
  1989	        The following field values are also preserved, as they are considered to be
  1990	        meaningful to the page as a whole, rather than to a specific revision:
  1991	
  1992	        * ``draft_title``
  1993	        * ``live``
  1994	        * ``has_unpublished_changes``
  1995	        * ``owner``
  1996	        * ``locked``
  1997	        * ``locked_by``
  1998	        * ``locked_at``
  1999	        * ``latest_revision``
  2000	        * ``latest_revision_created_at``
  2001	        * ``first_published_at``
  2002	        * ``alias_of``
  2003	        * ``wagtail_admin_comments`` (COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME)
  2004	        """
  2005	
  2006	        # Old revisions (pre Wagtail 2.15) may have saved comment data under the name 'comments'
  2007	        # rather than the current relation name as set by COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME;
  2008	        # if a 'comments' field exists and looks like our comments model, alter the data to use
  2009	        # COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME before restoring
  2010	        if (
  2011	            COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME not in content
  2012	            and "comments" in content
  2013	            and isinstance(content["comments"], list)
  2014	            and len(content["comments"])
  2015	            and isinstance(content["comments"][0], dict)
  2016	            and "contentpath" in content["comments"][0]
  2017	        ):
  2018	            content[COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME] = content["comments"]
  2019	            del content["comments"]
  2020	
  2021	        obj = self.specific_class.from_serializable_data(content)
  2022	
  2023	        # These should definitely never change between revisions
  2024	        obj.id = self.id
  2025	        obj.pk = self.pk
  2026	        obj.content_type_id = self.content_type_id
  2027	
  2028	        # Override possibly-outdated tree parameter fields
  2029	        obj.path = self.path
  2030	        obj.depth = self.depth
  2031	        obj.numchild = self.numchild
  2032	
  2033	        # Update url_path to reflect potential slug changes, but maintaining the page's
  2034	        # existing tree position
  2035	        obj.set_url_path(self.get_parent())
  2036	
  2037	        # Ensure other values that are meaningful for the page as a whole (rather than
  2038	        # to a specific revision) are preserved
  2039	        obj.draft_title = self.draft_title
  2040	        obj.live = self.live
  2041	        obj.has_unpublished_changes = self.has_unpublished_changes
  2042	        obj.owner_id = self.owner_id
  2043	        obj.locked = self.locked
  2044	        obj.locked_by_id = self.locked_by_id
  2045	        obj.locked_at = self.locked_at
  2046	        obj.latest_revision_id = self.latest_revision_id
  2047	        obj.latest_revision_created_at = self.latest_revision_created_at
  2048	
  2049	        if obj.first_published_at is None:
  2050	            obj.first_published_at = self.first_published_at
  2051	
  2052	        obj.translation_key = self.translation_key
  2053	        obj.locale_id = self.locale_id
  2054	        obj.alias_of_id = self.alias_of_id
  2055	        revision_comment_positions = dict(
  2056	            getattr(obj, COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME).values_list("id", "position")
  2057	        )
  2058	        page_comments = (
  2059	            getattr(self, COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME)
  2060	            .filter(resolved_at__isnull=True)
  2061	            .defer("position")
  2062	        )
  2063	        for comment in page_comments:
  2064	            # attempt to retrieve the comment position from the revision's stored version
  2065	            # of the comment
  2066	            try:
  2067	                comment.position = revision_comment_positions[comment.id]
  2068	            except KeyError:
  2069	                pass
  2070	        setattr(obj, COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME, page_comments)
  2071	
  2072	        return obj

  2074	    @property
  2075	    def has_workflow(self):
  2076	        """
  2077	        Returns ``True`` if the page or an ancestor has an active workflow assigned, otherwise ``False``.
  2078	        """
  2079	        if not getattr(settings, "WAGTAIL_WORKFLOW_ENABLED", True):
  2080	            return False
  2081	        return (
  2082	            self.get_ancestors(inclusive=True)
  2083	            .filter(workflowpage__isnull=False)
  2084	            .filter(workflowpage__workflow__active=True)
  2085	            .exists()
  2086	        )

  2088	    def get_workflow(self):
  2089	        """
  2090	        Returns the active workflow assigned to the page or its nearest ancestor.
  2091	        """
  2092	        if not getattr(settings, "WAGTAIL_WORKFLOW_ENABLED", True):
  2093	            return None
  2094	
  2095	        if hasattr(self, "workflowpage") and self.workflowpage.workflow.active:
  2096	            return self.workflowpage.workflow
  2097	        else:
  2098	            try:
  2099	                workflow = (
  2100	                    self.get_ancestors()
  2101	                    .filter(workflowpage__isnull=False)
  2102	                    .filter(workflowpage__workflow__active=True)
  2103	                    .order_by("-depth")
  2104	                    .first()
  2105	                    .workflowpage.workflow
  2106	                )
  2107	            except AttributeError:
  2108	                workflow = None
  2109	            return workflow

  2133	class GroupPagePermissionManager(models.Manager):
  2134	    def create(self, **kwargs):
  2135	        # Simplify creation of GroupPagePermission objects by allowing one
  2136	        # of permission or permission_type to be passed in.
  2137	        permission = kwargs.get("permission")
  2138	        permission_type = kwargs.pop("permission_type", None)
  2139	        if not permission and permission_type:
  2140	            kwargs["permission"] = Permission.objects.get(
  2141	                content_type=get_default_page_content_type(),
  2142	                codename=f"{permission_type}_page",
  2143	            )
  2144	        return super().create(**kwargs)

  2567	                path__startswith=path, depth__gte=depth

The admin layer is the largest surface: explorer/listing, edit/create/copy/move, privacy, workflow assignments, chooser widgets, home dashboard, reports, and page-specific forms all touch the contract. I’m going to treat repeated methods in the same class as separate dependents only when they represent distinct code paths or schema assumptions, per your flat-list requirement.

   173	    def get_valid_orderings(self):
   174	        valid_orderings = super().get_valid_orderings()
   175	
   176	        if self.is_searching:
   177	            # ordering by content type not currently available when searching, due to
   178	            # https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/issues/6616
   179	            try:
   180	                valid_orderings.remove("content_type")
   181	                valid_orderings.remove("-content_type")
   182	            except ValueError:

   184	
   185	        return valid_orderings

   187	    def get_ordering(self):
   188	        if self.is_searching and not self.is_explicitly_ordered:
   189	            # default to ordering by relevance
   190	            default_ordering = None
   191	        else:
   192	            default_ordering = self.default_ordering
   193	
   194	        ordering = self.request.GET.get("ordering", default_ordering)
   195	        if ordering not in self.get_valid_orderings():
   196	            ordering = default_ordering
   197

   200	    def annotate_queryset(self, pages):
   201	        pages = pages.prefetch_related("content_type", "sites_rooted_here")
   202	
   203	        # We want specific page instances, but do not need streamfield values here
   204	        pages = pages.defer_streamfields().specific()
   205	
   206	        # Annotate queryset with various states to be used later for performance optimisations
   207	        if getattr(settings, "WAGTAIL_WORKFLOW_ENABLED", True):
   208	            pages = pages.prefetch_workflow_states()
   209	        if self.i18n_enabled:
   210	            pages = pages.prefetch_related("locale").annotate_has_untranslated_locale()
   211	
   212	        pages = pages.annotate_site_root_state().annotate_approved_schedule()
   213	
   214	        return pages

   216	    def order_queryset(self, queryset):
   217	        if self.is_searching and not self.is_explicitly_ordered:
   218	            # search backend will order by relevance in this case, so don't bother to
   219	            # apply an ordering on the queryset
   220	            return queryset
   221	
   222	        if self.ordering == "ord":
   223	            # preserve the native ordering from get_children()
   224	            pass
   225	        elif self.ordering == "latest_revision_created_at" and not self.is_searching:
   226	            # order by oldest revision first.
   227	            # Special case NULL entries - these should go at the top of the list.
   228	            # Skip this special case when searching (and fall through to plain field ordering
   229	            # instead) as search backends do not support F objects in order_by
   230	            queryset = queryset.order_by(
   231	                F("latest_revision_created_at").asc(nulls_first=True)
   232	            )
   233	        elif self.ordering == "-latest_revision_created_at" and not self.is_searching:
   234	            # order by oldest revision first.
   235	            # Special case NULL entries - these should go at the end of the list.
   236	            # Skip this special case when searching (and fall through to plain field ordering
   237	            # instead) as search backends do not support F objects in order_by
   238	            queryset = queryset.order_by(
   239	                F("latest_revision_created_at").desc(nulls_last=True)
   240	            )
   241	        else:
   242	            queryset = super().order_queryset(queryset)
   243	
   244	        return queryset

   249	                self.search_query, order_by_relevance=(not self.is_explicitly_ordered)
   250	            )
   251	
   252	        return queryset

   454	        if self.permissions.can_view_revisions():
   455	            return reverse("wagtailadmin_pages:history", args=[self.parent_page.id])
   456	
   457	    def get_reorder_url(self):
   458	        return reverse("wagtailadmin_pages:set_page_position", args=[999999])
   459	
   460	    def get_table_kwargs(self):
   461	        kwargs = super().get_table_kwargs()
   462	        kwargs["parent_page"] = self.parent_page
   463	        return kwargs
   464	
   465	    def get_ordering(self):
   466	        if self.is_searching and not self.is_explicitly_ordered:
   467	            # default to ordering by relevance
   468	            default_ordering = None
   469	        else:
   470	            default_ordering = (
   471	                self.default_ordering or self.parent_page.get_admin_default_ordering()
   472	            )
   473	
   474	        ordering = self.request.GET.get("ordering", default_ordering)
   475	        if ordering not in self.get_valid_orderings():
   476	            ordering = default_ordering
   477	
   478	        return ordering
   479	
   480	    def get_page_subtitle(self):
   481	        return self.parent_page.get_admin_display_title()
   482	
   483	    def get_context_data(self, **kwargs):
   484	        context = super().get_context_data(**kwargs)
   485	
   486	        if self.is_searching:
   487	            Page.objects.annotate_parent_page(context["object_list"])
   488	            for page in context["object_list"]:
   489	                # add annotation if parent page is found and is not the currently viewed parent
   490	                # to be used by PageTitleColumn instead of a dedicated ParentPageColumn
   491	                if page._parent_page and page._parent_page != self.parent_page:
   492	                    page.annotated_parent_page = page._parent_page
   493	
   494	        context.update(
   495	            {
   496	                "parent_page": self.parent_page,
   497	                "history_url": self.get_history_url(),
   498	                "is_searching_whole_tree": self.is_searching_whole_tree,
   499	            }
   500	        )
   501	
   502	        if not self.results_only:
   503	            side_panels = self.get_side_panels()
   504	            context["side_panels"] = side_panels
   505	            context["media"] += side_panels.media
   506	
   507	        return context
   508	
   509	    def get_side_panels(self):
   510	        # Don't show side panels on the root page
   511	        if self.parent_page.is_root():
   512	            return MediaContainer()
   513	
   514	        side_panels = [
   515	            PageStatusSidePanel(
   516	                self.parent_page.get_latest_revision_as_object(),
   517	                self.request,
   518	                show_schedule_publishing_toggle=False,
   519	                live_object=self.parent_page,
   520	                scheduled_object=self.scheduled_page,
   521	                locale=self.locale,
   522	                translations=self.translations,
   523	            ),
   524	        ]
   525	        return MediaContainer(side_panels)
   526	
   527	    def get_translations(self):
   528	        return [
   529	            {
   530	                "locale": translation.locale,
   531	                "url": reverse(self.index_url_name, args=[translation.id]),
   532	            }
   533	            for translation in self.parent_page.get_translations()
   534	            .only("id", "locale")
   535	            .select_related("locale")
   536	        ]

    40	class AddSubpageView(TemplateView):
    41	    template_name = "wagtailadmin/pages/add_subpage.html"
    42	
    43	    def dispatch(self, request, parent_page_id, *args, **kwargs):
    44	        self.parent_page = get_object_or_404(Page, id=parent_page_id).specific
    45	        if not self.parent_page.permissions_for_user(request.user).can_add_subpage():
    46	            raise PermissionDenied
    47	
    48	        self.page_types = [
    49	            (
    50	                model.get_verbose_name(),
    51	                model._meta.app_label,
    52	                model._meta.model_name,
    53	                model.get_page_description(),
    54	            )
    55	            for model in type(self.parent_page).creatable_subpage_models()
    56	            if model.can_create_at(self.parent_page)
    57	        ]
    58	        # sort by lower-cased version of verbose name
    59	        self.page_types.sort(key=lambda page_type: page_type[0].lower())
    60	
    61	        if len(self.page_types) == 1:
    62	            # Only one page type is available - redirect straight to the create form rather than
    63	            # making the user choose
    64	            verbose_name, app_label, model_name, description = self.page_types[0]
    65	            return redirect(
    66	                "wagtailadmin_pages:add", app_label, model_name, self.parent_page.id
    67	            )
    68	        return super().dispatch(request, parent_page_id, *args, **kwargs)
    69	
    70	    def get_context_data(self, **kwargs):
    71	        context = super().get_context_data(**kwargs)
    72	        context.update(
    73	            {
    74	                "parent_page": self.parent_page,
    75	                "page_types": self.page_types,
    76	                "next": get_valid_next_url_from_request(self.request),
    77	            }
    78	        )
    79	        return context

    89	    def dispatch(
    90	        self, request, content_type_app_name, content_type_model_name, parent_page_id
    91	    ):
    92	        self.parent_page = get_object_or_404(Page, id=parent_page_id).specific
    93	        self.parent_page_perms = self.parent_page.permissions_for_user(
    94	            self.request.user
    95	        )
    96	        if not self.parent_page_perms.can_add_subpage():
    97	            raise PermissionDenied
    98	
    99	        try:
   100	            self.page_content_type = ContentType.objects.get_by_natural_key(
   101	                content_type_app_name, content_type_model_name
   102	            )
   103	        except ContentType.DoesNotExist as e:
   104	            raise Http404 from e
   105	
   106	        # Get class
   107	        self.page_class = self.page_content_type.model_class()
   108	
   109	        # Make sure the class is a descendant of Page
   110	        if not issubclass(self.page_class, Page):
   111	            raise Http404
   112	
   113	        # page must be in the list of allowed subpage types for this parent ID
   114	        if self.page_class not in self.parent_page.creatable_subpage_models():
   115	            raise PermissionDenied
   116	
   117	        if not self.page_class.can_create_at(self.parent_page):
   118	            raise PermissionDenied
   119	
   120	        response = self.run_hook(
   121	            "before_create_page", self.request, self.parent_page, self.page_class
   122	        )
   123	        if response:
   124	            if self.expects_json_response and not self.response_is_json(response):
   125	                # Hook response is not suitable for a JSON response, so construct our own error response
   126	                return self.json_error_response(
   127	                    "blocked_by_hook",
   128	                    _("Request to create %(model_name)s was blocked by hook.")
   129	                    % {"model_name": Page._meta.verbose_name},
   130	                )
   131	            else:
   132	                return response
   133	
   134	        self.locale = self.parent_page.locale
   135	        self.page = self.page_class(owner=self.request.user)
   136	        self.page.locale = self.locale
   137	
   138	        if getattr(settings, "WAGTAIL_I18N_ENABLED", False):
   139	            # If the parent page is the root page. The user may specify any locale they like
   140	            if self.parent_page.is_root():
   141	                selected_locale = request.GET.get("locale", None) or request.POST.get(
   142	                    "locale", None
   143	                )
   144	                if selected_locale:
   145	                    self.locale = get_object_or_404(
   146	                        Locale, language_code=selected_locale
   147	                    )
   148	            self.page.locale = self.locale
   149	            self.translations = self.get_translations()
   150	        else:
   151	            self.locale = None
   152	            self.translations = []
   153	
   154	        self.edit_handler = self.page_class.get_edit_handler()
   155	        self.form_class = self.edit_handler.get_form_class()
   156	
   157	        # Note: Comment notifications should be enabled by default for pages that a user creates
   158	        self.subscription = PageSubscription(
   159	            page=self.page, user=self.request.user, comment_notifications=True
   160	        )
   161	
   162	        self.next_url = get_valid_next_url_from_request(self.request)
   163	
   164	        self.autosave_interval = getattr(settings, "WAGTAIL_AUTOSAVE_INTERVAL", 500)
   165	        self.autosave_enabled = self.autosave_interval > 0
   166	
   167	        return super().dispatch(request)

   169	    def post(self, request):

   187	    @cached_property

   232	    def set_default_privacy_setting(self):
   233	        # privacy setting options BaseViewRestriction.RESTRICTION_CHOICES
   234	        default_privacy_setting = self.page.get_default_privacy_setting(self.request)
   235	
   236	        if default_privacy_setting["type"] == BaseViewRestriction.NONE:
   237	            # default privacy setting is public no need to do anything
   238	            pass
   239	        elif default_privacy_setting["type"] == BaseViewRestriction.LOGIN:
   240	            PageViewRestriction.objects.create(
   241	                page=self.page,
   242	                restriction_type=BaseViewRestriction.LOGIN,
   243	            )
   244	        elif default_privacy_setting["type"] == BaseViewRestriction.PASSWORD:
   245	            PageViewRestriction.objects.create(
   246	                page=self.page,
   247	                restriction_type=BaseViewRestriction.PASSWORD,
   248	                password=default_privacy_setting["password"],
   249	            )
   250	        elif default_privacy_setting["type"] == BaseViewRestriction.GROUPS:
   251	            # Create a page view restriction for groups
   252	            groups_page_restriction = PageViewRestriction.objects.create(
   253	                page=self.page,
   254	                restriction_type=BaseViewRestriction.GROUPS,
   255	            )
   256	            # add groups to the page view restriction
   257	            groups_page_restriction.groups.set(default_privacy_setting["groups"])
   258	        else:
   259	            raise ValueError(
   260	                f"Invalid privacy setting {default_privacy_setting.get('type')!r}"
   261	            )

   263	    def save_action(self):
   264	        self.page = self.form.save(commit=False)
   265	        self.page.live = False
   266	
   267	        # Save page
   268	        self.parent_page.add_child(instance=self.page)
   269	
   270	        # Set page privacy setting
   271	        self.set_default_privacy_setting()
   272	
   273	        # Save revision
   274	        revision = self.page.save_revision(
   275	            user=self.request.user, log_action=True, clean=False
   276	        )
   277	
   278	        # Save subscription settings
   279	        self.subscription.page = self.page
   280	        self.subscription.save()
   281	
   282	        if not self.expects_json_response:
   283	            # Notification
   284	            messages.success(
   285	                self.request,
   286	                _("Page '%(page_title)s' created.")
   287	                % {"page_title": self.page.get_admin_display_title()},
   288	            )
   289	
   290	        response = self.run_hook("after_create_page", self.request, self.page)
   291	        if response:
   292	            if self.expects_json_response and not self.response_is_json(response):
   293	                # Hook response is not suitable for a JSON response, so ignore it and just use
   294	                # the standard one
   295	                pass
   296	            else:
   297	                return response
   298	
   299	        if self.expects_json_response:
   300	            edit_url = self.get_edit_url()
   301	            hydrate_url = set_query_params(edit_url, {"_w_hydrate_create_view": "1"})
   302	            return JsonResponse(
   303	                {
   304	                    "success": True,
   305	                    "pk": self.page.pk,
   306	                    "revision_id": revision.pk,
   307	                    "revision_created_at": revision.created_at.isoformat(),
   308	                    "field_updates": dict(self.form.get_field_updates_for_resave()),
   309	                    "comments": self.form.serialize_comments(self.request.user),
   310	                    "url": edit_url,
   311	                    "hydrate_url": hydrate_url,
   312	                }
   313	            )
   314	        else:
   315	            # remain on edit page for further edits
   316	            return self.redirect_and_remain()

   318	    def publish_action(self):
   319	        self.page = self.form.save(commit=False)
   320	
   321	        # Save page
   322	        self.parent_page.add_child(instance=self.page)
   323	
   324	        # Set page privacy setting
   325	        self.set_default_privacy_setting()
   326	
   327	        # Save revision
   328	        revision = self.page.save_revision(user=self.request.user, log_action=True)
   329	
   330	        # Save subscription settings
   331	        self.subscription.page = self.page
   332	        self.subscription.save()
   333	
   334	        # Publish
   335	        response = self.run_hook("before_publish_page", self.request, self.page)
   336	        if response:
   337	            return response
   338	
   339	        revision.publish(user=self.request.user)
   340	
   341	        # get a fresh copy so that any changes coming from revision.publish() are passed on
   342	        self.page.refresh_from_db()
   343	
   344	        response = self.run_hook("after_publish_page", self.request, self.page)
   345	        if response:
   346	            return response
   347	
   348	        # Notification
   349	        if self.page.go_live_at and self.page.go_live_at > timezone.now():
   350	            messages.success(
   351	                self.request,
   352	                _("Page '%(page_title)s' created and scheduled for publishing.")
   353	                % {"page_title": self.page.get_admin_display_title()},
   354	                buttons=[self.get_edit_message_button()],
   355	            )
   356	        else:
   357	            buttons = []
   358	            if self.page.url is not None:
   359	                buttons.append(self.get_view_live_message_button())
   360	            buttons.append(self.get_edit_message_button())
   361	            messages.success(
   362	                self.request,
   363	                _("Page '%(page_title)s' created and published.")
   364	                % {"page_title": self.page.get_admin_display_title()},
   365	                buttons=buttons,
   366	            )
   367	
   368	        response = self.run_hook("after_create_page", self.request, self.page)
   369	        if response:
   370	            return response
   371	
   372	        return self.redirect_away()

   374	    def submit_action(self):
   375	        self.page = self.form.save(commit=False)
   376	        self.page.live = False
   377	
   378	        # Save page
   379	        self.parent_page.add_child(instance=self.page)
   380	
   381	        # Set page privacy setting
   382	        self.set_default_privacy_setting()
   383	
   384	        # Save revision
   385	        self.page.save_revision(user=self.request.user, log_action=True)
   386	
   387	        # Submit
   388	        workflow = self.page.get_workflow()
   389	        workflow.start(self.page, self.request.user)
   390	
   391	        # Save subscription settings
   392	        self.subscription.page = self.page
   393	        self.subscription.save()
   394	
   395	        # Notification
   396	        buttons = []
   397	        if self.page.is_previewable():
   398	            buttons.append(self.get_view_draft_message_button())
   399	
   400	        buttons.append(self.get_edit_message_button())
   401	
   402	        messages.success(
   403	            self.request,
   404	            _("Page '%(page_title)s' created and submitted for moderation.")
   405	            % {"page_title": self.page.get_admin_display_title()},
   406	            buttons=buttons,
   407	        )
   408	
   409	        response = self.run_hook("after_create_page", self.request, self.page)
   410	        if response:
   411	            return response
   412	
   413	        return self.redirect_away()

   415	    def redirect_away(self):
   416	        if self.next_url:
   417	            # redirect back to 'next' url if present

   423	    def redirect_and_remain(self):
   424	        target_url = self.get_edit_url()

   426	            # Ensure the 'next' url is passed through again if present
   427	            target_url += "?next=%s" % quote(self.next_url)
   428	        return redirect(target_url)

    71	            return ["wagtailadmin/pages/edit.html"]
