    91	    def get_commenting_changes(self):
    92	        """
    93	        Finds comments that have been changed during this request.
    94	
    95	        Returns a tuple of 5 lists:
    96	         - New comments
    97	         - Deleted comments
    98	         - Resolved comments
    99	         - Edited comments
   100	         - Replied comments (dict containing the instance and list of replies)
   101	        """
   102	        # Get changes
   103	        comments_formset = self.form.formsets["comments"]
   104	        new_comments = comments_formset.new_objects
   105	        deleted_comments = comments_formset.deleted_objects
   106	
   107	        # Assume any changed comments that are resolved were only just resolved
   108	        resolved_comments = []
   109	        edited_comments = []
   110	        for changed_comment, changed_fields in comments_formset.changed_objects:
   111	            if changed_comment.resolved_at and "resolved" in changed_fields:
   112	                resolved_comments.append(changed_comment)
   113	
   114	            if "text" in changed_fields:
   115	                edited_comments.append(changed_comment)
   116	
   117	        new_replies = []
   118	        deleted_replies = []
   119	        edited_replies = []
   120	        for comment_form in comments_formset.forms:
   121	            # New
   122	            replies = getattr(comment_form.formsets["replies"], "new_objects", [])
   123	            if replies:
   124	                new_replies.append((comment_form.instance, replies))
   125	
   126	            # Deleted
   127	            replies = getattr(comment_form.formsets["replies"], "deleted_objects", [])
   128	            if replies:
   129	                deleted_replies.append((comment_form.instance, replies))
   130	
   131	            # Edited
   132	            replies = getattr(comment_form.formsets["replies"], "changed_objects", [])
   133	            replies = [
   134	                reply for reply, changed_fields in replies if "text" in changed_fields
   135	            ]
   136	            if replies:
   137	                edited_replies.append((comment_form.instance, replies))
   138	
   139	        return {
   140	            "new_comments": new_comments,
   141	            "deleted_comments": deleted_comments,
   142	            "resolved_comments": resolved_comments,
   143	            "edited_comments": edited_comments,
   144	            "new_replies": new_replies,
   145	            "deleted_replies": deleted_replies,
   146	            "edited_replies": edited_replies,
   147	        }
   148	
   149	    def send_commenting_notifications(self, changes):
   150	        """
   151	        Sends notifications about any changes to comments to anyone who is subscribed.
   152	        """
   153	        relevant_comment_ids = []
   154	        relevant_comment_ids.extend(
   155	            comment.pk for comment in changes["resolved_comments"]
   156	        )
   157	        relevant_comment_ids.extend(
   158	            comment.pk for comment, replies in changes["new_replies"]
   159	        )
   160	
   161	        # Skip if no changes were made
   162	        # Note: We don't email about edited comments so ignore those here
   163	        if (
   164	            not changes["new_comments"]
   165	            and not changes["deleted_comments"]
   166	            and not changes["resolved_comments"]
   167	            and not changes["new_replies"]
   168	        ):
   169	            return
   170	
   171	        # Get global page comment subscribers
   172	        subscribers = PageSubscription.objects.filter(
   173	            page=self.page, comment_notifications=True
   174	        ).select_related("user")
   175	        global_recipient_users = [
   176	            subscriber.user
   177	            for subscriber in subscribers
   178	            if subscriber.user != self.request.user
   179	        ]
   180	
   181	        # Get subscribers to individual threads
   182	        replies = CommentReply.objects.filter(comment_id__in=relevant_comment_ids)
   183	        comments = Comment.objects.filter(id__in=relevant_comment_ids)
   184	        thread_users = (
   185	            get_user_model()
   186	            .objects.exclude(pk=self.request.user.pk)
   187	            .exclude(pk__in=subscribers.values_list("user_id", flat=True))
   188	            .filter(
   189	                Q(comment_replies__comment_id__in=relevant_comment_ids)
   190	                | Q(**{("%s__pk__in" % COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME): relevant_comment_ids})
   191	            )
   192	            .prefetch_related(
   193	                Prefetch("comment_replies", queryset=replies),
   194	                Prefetch(COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME, queryset=comments),
   195	            )
   196	        )
   197	
   198	        # Skip if no recipients
   199	        if not (global_recipient_users or thread_users):
   200	            return
   201	        thread_users = [
   202	            (
   203	                user,
   204	                set(
   205	                    list(user.comment_replies.values_list("comment_id", flat=True))
   206	                    + list(
   207	                        getattr(user, COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME).values_list(
   208	                            "pk", flat=True
   209	                        )
   210	                    )
   211	                ),
   212	            )
   213	            for user in thread_users
   214	        ]
   215	        mailed_users = set()
   216	
   217	        for current_user, current_threads in thread_users:
   218	            # We are trying to avoid calling send_notification for each user for performance reasons
   219	            # so group the users receiving the same thread notifications together here
   220	            if current_user in mailed_users:
   221	                continue
   222	            users = [current_user]
   223	            mailed_users.add(current_user)
   224	            for user, threads in thread_users:
   225	                if user not in mailed_users and threads == current_threads:
   226	                    users.append(user)
   227	                    mailed_users.add(user)
   228	            send_notification(
   229	                users,
   230	                "updated_comments",
   231	                {
   232	                    "page": self.page,
   233	                    "editor": self.request.user,
   234	                    "new_comments": [
   235	                        comment
   236	                        for comment in changes["new_comments"]
   237	                        if comment.pk in threads
   238	                    ],
   239	                    "resolved_comments": [
   240	                        comment
   241	                        for comment in changes["resolved_comments"]
   242	                        if comment.pk in threads
   243	                    ],
   244	                    "deleted_comments": [],
   245	                    "replied_comments": [
   246	                        {
   247	                            "comment": comment,
   248	                            "replies": replies,
   249	                        }
   250	                        for comment, replies in changes["new_replies"]
   251	                        if comment.pk in threads
   252	                    ],
   253	                },
   254	            )
   255	
   256	        return send_notification(
   257	            global_recipient_users,
   258	            "updated_comments",
   259	            {
   260	                "page": self.page,
   261	                "editor": self.request.user,
   262	                "new_comments": changes["new_comments"],
   263	                "resolved_comments": changes["resolved_comments"],
   264	                "deleted_comments": changes["deleted_comments"],
   265	                "replied_comments": [
   266	                    {
   267	                        "comment": comment,
   268	                        "replies": replies,
   269	                    }
   270	                    for comment, replies in changes["new_replies"]
   271	                ],
   272	            },
   273	        )
   274	
   275	    def log_commenting_changes(self, changes, revision):
   276	        """
   277	        Generates log entries for any changes made to comments or replies.
   278	        """
   279	        for comment in changes["new_comments"]:
   280	            comment.log_create(page_revision=revision, user=self.request.user)
   281	
   282	        for comment in changes["edited_comments"]:
   283	            comment.log_edit(page_revision=revision, user=self.request.user)
   284	
   285	        for comment in changes["resolved_comments"]:
   286	            comment.log_resolve(page_revision=revision, user=self.request.user)
   287	
   288	        for comment in changes["deleted_comments"]:
   289	            comment.log_delete(page_revision=revision, user=self.request.user)
   290	
   291	        for comment, replies in changes["new_replies"]:
   292	            for reply in replies:
   293	                reply.log_create(page_revision=revision, user=self.request.user)
   294	
   295	        for comment, replies in changes["edited_replies"]:
   296	            for reply in replies:
   297	                reply.log_edit(page_revision=revision, user=self.request.user)
   298	
   299	        for comment, replies in changes["deleted_replies"]:
   300	            for reply in replies:
   301	                reply.log_delete(page_revision=revision, user=self.request.user)

   338	    def dispatch(self, request, page_id, **kwargs):
   339	        self.real_page_record = get_object_or_404(
   340	            Page.objects.prefetch_workflow_states(), id=page_id
   341	        )
   342	        self.latest_revision = self.real_page_record.get_latest_revision()
   343	        self.scheduled_revision = self.real_page_record.scheduled_revision
   344	        self.page_content_type = self.real_page_record.cached_content_type
   345	        self.page_class = self.real_page_record.specific_class
   346	
   347	        if self.page_class is None:
   348	            raise PageClassNotFoundError(
   349	                f"The page '{self.real_page_record}' cannot be edited because the "
   350	                f"model class used to create it ({self.page_content_type.app_label}."
   351	                f"{self.page_content_type.model}) can no longer be found in the codebase. "
   352	                "This usually happens as a result of switching between git "
   353	                "branches without running migrations to trigger the removal of "
   354	                "unused ContentTypes. To edit the page, you will need to switch "
   355	                "back to a branch where the model class is still present."
   356	            )
   357	
   358	        self.revision_id = kwargs.get("revision_id")
   359	        self.is_reverting = bool(self.revision_id)
   360	        self.previous_revision = None
   361	        if self.is_reverting:
   362	            self.previous_revision = get_object_or_404(
   363	                self.real_page_record.revisions, id=self.revision_id
   364	            )
   365	        self.page = self.get_object()
   366	        self.parent = self.page.get_parent()
   367	        self.scheduled_page = self.real_page_record.get_scheduled_revision_as_object()
   368	
   369	        self.page_perms = self.page.permissions_for_user(self.request.user)
   370	        self.lock = self.page.get_lock()
   371	        self.locked_for_user = self.lock is not None and self.lock.for_user(
   372	            self.request.user
   373	        )
   374	
   375	        if not self.page_perms.can_edit():
   376	            raise PermissionDenied
   377	
   378	        self.next_url = get_valid_next_url_from_request(self.request)
   379	
   380	        response = self.run_hook("before_edit_page", self.request, self.page)
   381	        if response:
   382	            if self.expects_json_response and not self.response_is_json(response):
   383	                # Hook response is not suitable for a JSON response, so construct our own error response
   384	                return self.json_error_response(
   385	                    "blocked_by_hook",
   386	                    _("Request to edit %(model_name)s was blocked by hook.")
   387	                    % {"model_name": Page._meta.verbose_name},
   388	                )
   389	            else:
   390	                return response
   391	
   392	        self.subscription, created = PageSubscription.objects.get_or_create(
   393	            page=self.page,
   394	            user=self.request.user,
   395	            defaults={
   396	                "comment_notifications": False,
   397	            },
   398	        )
   399	
   400	        self.edit_handler = self.page_class.get_edit_handler()
   401	        self.form_class = self.edit_handler.get_form_class()
   402	
   403	        if getattr(settings, "WAGTAIL_WORKFLOW_ENABLED", True):
   404	            # Retrieve current workflow state if set, default to last workflow state
   405	            self.workflow_state = (
   406	                self.page.current_workflow_state
   407	                or self.page.workflow_states.order_by("created_at").last()
   408	            )
   409	        else:
   410	            self.workflow_state = None
   411	
   412	        if getattr(settings, "WAGTAIL_I18N_ENABLED", False):
   413	            self.locale = self.page.locale
   414	            self.translations = self.get_translations()
   415	        else:
   416	            self.locale = None
   417	            self.translations = []
   418	
   419	        if self.workflow_state:
   420	            self.workflow_tasks = self.workflow_state.all_tasks_with_status()
   421	        else:
   422	            self.workflow_tasks = []
   423	
   424	        self.has_unsaved_changes = False
   425	        self.errors_debug = None
   426	        self.autosave_interval = getattr(settings, "WAGTAIL_AUTOSAVE_INTERVAL", 500)
   427	        self.autosave_enabled = self.autosave_interval > 0
   428	
   429	        return super().dispatch(request, page_id, **kwargs)

   431	    def get(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
   432	        if self.lock:
   433	            lock_message = self.lock.get_message(self.request.user)
   434	            if lock_message:
   435	                if isinstance(self.lock, BasicLock) and self.page_perms.can_unlock():
   436	                    lock_message = format_html(
   437	                        '{} <span class="buttons"><button type="button" class="button button-small button-secondary" data-action="w-action#post" data-controller="w-action" data-w-action-url-value="{}">{}</button></span>',
   438	                        lock_message,
   439	                        reverse("wagtailadmin_pages:unlock", args=(self.page.id,)),
   440	                        _("Unlock"),
   441	                    )
   442	
   443	                if (
   444	                    isinstance(self.lock, ScheduledForPublishLock)
   445	                    and self.page_perms.can_unschedule()
   446	                ):
   447	                    lock_message = format_html(
   448	                        '{} <span class="buttons"><button type="button" class="button button-small button-secondary" data-action="w-action#post" data-controller="w-action" data-w-action-url-value="{}">{}</button></span>',
   449	                        lock_message,
   450	                        reverse(
   451	                            "wagtailadmin_pages:revisions_unschedule",
   452	                            args=[self.page.id, self.scheduled_revision.pk],
   453	                        ),
   454	                        _("Cancel scheduled publish"),
   455	                    )
   456	
   457	                if (
   458	                    not isinstance(self.lock, ScheduledForPublishLock)
   459	                    and self.locked_for_user
   460	                ):
   461	                    messages.warning(self.request, lock_message, extra_tags="lock")
   462	                else:
   463	                    messages.info(self.request, lock_message, extra_tags="lock")
   464	
   465	        self.form = self.form_class(
   466	            instance=self.page,
   467	            subscription=self.subscription,
   468	            parent_page=self.parent,
   469	            for_user=self.request.user,
   470	        )
   471	
   472	        return self.render_to_response(self.get_context_data())

   500	    @cached_property
   501	    def latest_revision_created_at(self):
   502	        return self.latest_revision and self.latest_revision.created_at.isoformat()
   503	
   504	    @cached_property
   505	    def is_out_of_date(self):
   506	        if not self.latest_revision:
   507	            return False
   508	
   509	        latest_revision_id = str(self.latest_revision.pk)
   510	
   511	        # Two different sessions cannot have the same autosave revision, so if
   512	        # autosave revision is present, it is either the latest or it is not.
   513	        if overwrite_revision_id := self.request.POST.get("overwrite_revision_id"):
   514	            return overwrite_revision_id != latest_revision_id
   515	
   516	        # Client has not made an autosave revision, check the loaded revision.
   517	        if loaded_revision_id := self.request.POST.get("loaded_revision_id"):
   518	            # If the loaded revision is not the latest revision, it is outdated.
   519	            if loaded_revision_id != latest_revision_id:
   520	                return True
   521	
   522	            # It's pointing to the latest revision, but that revision may have
   523	            # been overwritten by another session (via autosave) since the editor
   524	            # loaded it. The created_at is strictly increasing, so assume the
   525	            # loaded revision outdated if it doesn't match the latest created_at.
   526	            return (
   527	                self.request.POST.get("loaded_revision_created_at", "")
   528	                != self.latest_revision_created_at
   529	            )
   530	
   531	        # Not enough information to deduce, assume it's up to date.
   532	        return False

   534	    def post(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
   535	        # Don't allow POST requests if the page is an alias
   536	        if self.page.alias_of_id:
   537	            # Return 405 "Method Not Allowed" response
   538	            return HttpResponse(status=405)
   539	
   540	        # For autosave, we only want to save the page if there are no conflicts
   541	        if self.expects_json_response and self.is_out_of_date:
   542	            return self.json_error_response(
   543	                "invalid_revision",
   544	                _("Saving will overwrite a newer version."),
   545	            )
   546	
   547	        self.form = self.form_class(
   548	            self.request.POST,
   549	            self.request.FILES,
   550	            instance=self.page,
   551	            subscription=self.subscription,
   552	            parent_page=self.parent,
   553	            for_user=self.request.user,
   554	        )
   555	
   556	        if self.action_name == "save":
   557	            self.form.defer_required_fields()
   558	
   559	        if self.form.is_valid() and not self.locked_for_user:
   560	            return self.form_valid(self.form)
   561	        else:
   562	            self.form.restore_required_fields()
   563	            return self.form_invalid(self.form)
   564	
   565	    def workflow_action_is_valid(self):
   566	        # The workflow might have been cancelled/ended after the page was loaded
   567	        workflow_task = self.page.current_workflow_task
   568	        if not workflow_task:
   569	            return False
   570	        self.workflow_action = self.request.POST.get("workflow-action-name")
   571	        available_actions = workflow_task.get_actions(self.page, self.request.user)
   572	        available_action_names = [
   573	            name for name, verbose_name, modal in available_actions
   574	        ]
   575	        return self.workflow_action in available_action_names
   576	
   577	    @cached_property
   578	    def action_name_and_method(self):
   579	        if self.request.POST.get("action-publish") and self.page_perms.can_publish():
   580	            return ("publish", self.publish_action)
   581	        elif (
   582	            self.request.POST.get("action-submit")
   583	            and self.page_perms.can_submit_for_moderation()
   584	        ):
   585	            return ("submit", self.submit_action)
   586	        elif (
   587	            self.request.POST.get("action-restart-workflow")
   588	            and self.page_perms.can_submit_for_moderation()
   589	            and self.workflow_state
   590	            and self.workflow_state.user_can_cancel(self.request.user)
   591	        ):
   592	            return ("restart_workflow", self.restart_workflow_action)
   593	        elif (
   594	            self.request.POST.get("action-workflow-action")
   595	            and self.workflow_action_is_valid()
   596	        ):
   597	            return ("perform_workflow_action", self.perform_workflow_action)
   598	        elif self.is_cancelling_workflow:
   599	            return ("cancel_workflow", self.cancel_workflow_action)
   600	        else:
   601	            return ("save", self.save_action)
   602	
   603	    @property
   604	    def action_name(self):
   605	        return self.action_name_and_method[0]
   606	
   607	    @property
   608	    def action_method(self):
   609	        return self.action_name_and_method[1]
   610	
   611	    def form_valid(self, form):
   612	        self.has_content_changes = self.form.has_changed()
   613	        return self.action_method()
   614	
   615	    def save_action(self):
   616	        try:
   617	            with transaction.atomic():
   618	                self.page = self.form.save(commit=not self.page.live)
   619	                self.subscription.save()
   620	
   621	                overwrite_revision_id = self.request.POST.get("overwrite_revision_id")
   622	                if overwrite_revision_id:
   623	                    try:
   624	                        overwrite_revision = self.page.revisions.get(
   625	                            pk=overwrite_revision_id
   626	                        )
   627	                    except Revision.DoesNotExist as e:
   628	                        raise PermissionDenied(
   629	                            "Cannot overwrite a revision that does not exist."
   630	                        ) from e
   631	                else:
   632	                    overwrite_revision = None
   633	
   634	                # Save revision
   635	                revision = self.page.save_revision(
   636	                    user=self.request.user,
   637	                    log_action=True,  # Always log the new revision on edit
   638	                    previous_revision=self.previous_revision,
   639	                    overwrite_revision=overwrite_revision,

   642	        except PermissionDenied as e:

   650	
   651	        if not self.expects_json_response:
   652	            self.add_save_confirmation_message()
   653	
   654	        if self.has_content_changes and "comments" in self.form.formsets:
   655	            changes = self.get_commenting_changes()

   657	            self.send_commenting_notifications(changes)

   659	        response = self.run_hook("after_edit_page", self.request, self.page)
   660	        if response:

   663	                # the standard one

   684	    def publish_action(self):
   685	        self.page = self.form.save(commit=not self.page.live)
   686	        self.subscription.save()

   689	        revision = self.page.save_revision(
   690	            user=self.request.user,
   691	            log_action=True,  # Always log the new revision on edit
   692	            previous_revision=self.previous_revision,
   693	        )
   694	
   695	        # store submitted go_live_at for messaging below
   696	        go_live_at = self.page.go_live_at
   697	
   698	        response = self.run_hook("before_publish_page", self.request, self.page)
   699	        if response:
   700	            return response
   701	
   702	        action = PublishPageRevisionAction(
   703	            revision,
   704	            user=self.request.user,
   705	            changed=self.has_content_changes,
   706	            previous_revision=self.previous_revision,
   707	        )
   708	        action.execute(skip_permission_checks=True)
   709	
   710	        if self.has_content_changes and "comments" in self.form.formsets:
   711	            changes = self.get_commenting_changes()
   712	            self.log_commenting_changes(changes, revision)
   713	            self.send_commenting_notifications(changes)
   714	
   715	        # Need to reload the page because the URL may have changed, and we
   716	        # need the up-to-date URL for the "View Live" button.
   717	        self.page = self.page.specific_class.objects.get(pk=self.page.pk)
   718	
   719	        response = self.run_hook("after_publish_page", self.request, self.page)
   720	        if response:
   721	            return response
   722	
   723	        # Notifications

   725	            # Page has been scheduled for publishing in the future

   727	            if self.is_reverting:

   729	                    "Version from %(previous_revision_datetime)s "

   731	                ) % {
   732	                    "previous_revision_datetime": render_timestamp(
   733	                        self.previous_revision.created_at
   734	                    ),
   735	                    "page_title": self.page.get_admin_display_title(),
   736	                }
   737	            else:
   738	                if self.page.live:
   739	                    message = _(
   740	                        "Page '%(page_title)s' is live and this version has been scheduled for publishing."
   741	                    ) % {"page_title": self.page.get_admin_display_title()}
   742	
   743	                else:
   744	                    message = _(
   745	                        "Page '%(page_title)s' has been scheduled for publishing."
   746	                    ) % {"page_title": self.page.get_admin_display_title()}
   747	
   748	            messages.success(
   749	                self.request, message, buttons=[self.get_edit_message_button()]
   750	            )
   751	
   752	        else:
   753	            # Page is being published now
   754	
   755	            if self.is_reverting:
   756	                message = _(
   757	                    "Version from %(datetime)s of page '%(page_title)s' has been published."
   758	                ) % {
   759	                    "datetime": render_timestamp(self.previous_revision.created_at),
   760	                    "page_title": self.page.get_admin_display_title(),

   762	            else:

   765	                }
   766	
   767	            buttons = []

   770	            buttons.append(self.get_edit_message_button())
   771	            messages.success(self.request, message, buttons=buttons)
   772	
   773	        response = self.run_hook("after_edit_page", self.request, self.page)
   774	        if response:

   777	        # we're done here - redirect back to the explorer

   780	    def submit_action(self):
   781	        self.page = self.form.save(commit=not self.page.live)

   784	        # Save revision

   795

   827	    def restart_workflow_action(self):
   828	        self.page = self.form.save(commit=not self.page.live)

   869	    def perform_workflow_action(self):

    14	class CopyForm(forms.Form):
    15	    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
    16	        # CopyPage must be passed a 'page' kwarg indicating the page to be copied
    17	        self.page = kwargs.pop("page")
    18	        self.user = kwargs.pop("user")
    19	        can_publish = page_permission_policy.user_has_permission(self.user, "publish")
    20	
    21	        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
    22	        self.fields["new_title"] = forms.CharField(
    23	            initial=self.page.title, label=_("New title")

    44	        subpage_count = pages_to_copy.count() - 1

    46	            self.fields["copy_subpages"] = forms.BooleanField(
    47	                required=False,
    48	                initial=True,
    49	                label=_("Copy subpages"),
    50	                help_text=ngettext(

    54	                )

    57	
    58	        if can_publish:

    60	            if pages_to_publish_count > 0:
    61	                # In the specific case that there are no subpages, customise the field label and help text
    62	                if subpage_count == 0:
    63	                    label = _("Publish copied page")
    64	                    help_text = _(
    65	                        "This page is live. Would you like to publish its copy as well?"
    66	                    )
    67	                else:

    69	                    help_text = ngettext(

    74	
    75	                self.fields["publish_copies"] = forms.BooleanField(
    76	                    required=False, initial=False, label=label, help_text=help_text
    77	                )
    78	
    79	            # Note that only users who can publish in the new parent page can create an alias.
    80	            # This is because alias pages must always match their original page's state.
    81	            self.fields["alias"] = forms.BooleanField(

    88	    def clean(self):
    89	        cleaned_data = super().clean()
    90	
    91	        # Make sure the slug isn't already in use
    92	        slug = cleaned_data.get("new_slug")
    93	
    94	        # New parent page given in form or parent of source, if parent_page is empty
    95	        parent_page = cleaned_data.get("new_parent_page") or self.page.get_parent()
    96	
    97	        # creating an alias requires publish permission
    98	        is_publishing = cleaned_data.get("publish_copies") or cleaned_data.get("alias")
    99	
   100	        # If the user requested to publish the copies, check they're allowed to publish at the destination
   101	        if (
   102	            is_publishing
   103	            and not parent_page.permissions_for_user(self.user).can_publish_subpage()
   104	        ):
   105	            self._errors["new_parent_page"] = self.error_class(
   106	                [
   107	                    _('You do not have permission to publish pages at "%(page_title)s"')
   108	                    % {
   109	                        "page_title": parent_page.specific_deferred.get_admin_display_title()
   110	                    }
   111	                ]
   112	            )
   113	
   114	        # check if user is allowed to copy the pages.
   115	        if not self.page.permissions_for_user(self.user).can_copy_to(parent_page):
   116	            self._errors["new_parent_page"] = self.error_class(
   117	                [
   118	                    _('You do not have permission to copy to page "%(page_title)s"')
   119	                    % {
   120	                        "page_title": parent_page.specific_deferred.get_admin_display_title()
   121	                    }
   122	                ]
   123	            )
   124	
   125	        # Count the pages with the same slug within the context of our copy's parent page
   126	        if slug and parent_page.get_children().filter(slug=slug).count():
   127	            self._errors["new_slug"] = self.error_class(
   128	                [
   129	                    _(
   130	                        'This slug is already in use within the context of its parent page "%(parent_page_title)s"'
   131	                    )
   132	                    % {"parent_page_title": parent_page}
   133	                ]
   134	            )
   135	            # The slug is no longer valid, hence remove it from cleaned_data
   136	            del cleaned_data["new_slug"]
   137	
   138	        # Don't allow recursive copies into self
   139	        if cleaned_data.get("copy_subpages") and (
   140	            self.page == parent_page or parent_page.is_descendant_of(self.page)
   141	        ):
   142	            self._errors["new_parent_page"] = self.error_class(
   143	                [_("You cannot copy a page into itself when copying subpages")]
   144	            )
   145	
   146	        return cleaned_data

    21	class TaskChooserSearchForm(forms.Form):
    22	    q = forms.CharField(
    23	        label=gettext_lazy("Search term"), widget=forms.TextInput(), required=False
    24	    )
    25	
    26	    def __init__(self, *args, task_type_choices=None, **kwargs):
    27	        placeholder = kwargs.pop("placeholder", _("Search…"))
    28	        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
    29	        self.fields["q"].widget.attrs = {"placeholder": placeholder}
    30	
    31	        # Add task type filter if there is more than one task type option
    32	        if task_type_choices and len(task_type_choices) > 1:
    33	            self.fields["task_type"] = forms.ChoiceField(
    34	                choices=(
    35	                    # Append an "All types" choice to the beginning
    36	                    [(None, _("All types"))]
    37	                    # The task type choices that are passed in use the models as values, we need
    38	                    # to convert these to something that can be represented in HTML
    39	                    + [
    40	                        (get_model_string(model), verbose_name)
    41	                        for model, verbose_name in task_type_choices
    42	                    ]
    43	                ),
    44	                required=False,
    45	            )
    46	
    47	        # Save a mapping of task_type values back to the model that we can reference later
    48	        self.task_type_choices = {
    49	            get_model_string(model): model for model, verbose_name in task_type_choices
    50	        }
    51	
    52	    def is_searching(self):
    53	        """
    54	        Returns True if the user typed a search query
    55	        """
    56	        return self.is_valid() and bool(self.cleaned_data.get("q"))
    57	
    58	    @cached_property
    59	    def task_model(self):
    60	        """
    61	        Returns the selected task model.
    62	
    63	        This looks for the task model in the following order:
    64	         1) If there's only one task model option, return it
    65	         2) If a task model has been selected, return it
    66	         3) Return the generic Task model
    67	        """
    68	        models = list(self.task_type_choices.values())
    69	        if len(models) == 1:
    70	            return models[0]
    71	
    72	        elif self.is_valid():
    73	            model_name = self.cleaned_data.get("task_type")
    74	            if model_name and model_name in self.task_type_choices:
    75	                return self.task_type_choices[model_name]
    76	
    77	        return Task
    78	
    79	    def specific_task_model_selected(self):
    80	        return self.task_model is not Task

    93	    def clean(self):
    94	        page = self.cleaned_data.get("page")
    95	        try:
    96	            existing_workflow = page.workflowpage.workflow
    97	            if not self.errors and existing_workflow != self.cleaned_data["workflow"]:
    98	                # If the form has no errors, Page has an existing Workflow assigned, that Workflow is not
    99	                # the selected Workflow, and overwrite_existing is not True, add a new error. This should be used to
   100	                # trigger the confirmation message in the view. This is why this error is only added if there are no
   101	                # other errors - confirmation should be the final step.
   102	                self.add_error(
   103	                    "page",
   104	                    ValidationError(
   105	                        _(
   106	                            "This page already has workflow '%(workflow_name)s' assigned."
   107	                        )
   108	                        % {"workflow_name": existing_workflow},
   109	                        code="existing_workflow",
   110	                    ),
   111	                )
   112	        except AttributeError:
   113	            pass

   116	        page = self.cleaned_data["page"]
   117	
   118	        if commit:
   119	            WorkflowPage.objects.update_or_create(
   120	                page=page,
   121	                defaults={"workflow": self.cleaned_data["workflow"]},

   125	class BaseWorkflowPagesFormSet(BaseFormSetMixin, forms.BaseInlineFormSet):
   126	    def clean(self):
   127	        """Checks that no two forms refer to the same page object"""
   128	        if any(self.errors):
   129	            # Don't bother validating the formset unless each form is valid on its own
   130	            return
   131	
   132	        pages = [
   133	            form.cleaned_data["page"]
   134	            for form in self.forms
   135	            # need to check for presence of 'page' in cleaned_data,
   136	            # because a completely blank form passes validation
   137	            if form not in self.deleted_forms and "page" in form.cleaned_data
   138	        ]
   139	        if len(set(pages)) != len(pages):
   140	            # pages list contains duplicates
   141	            raise forms.ValidationError(
   142	                _("You cannot assign this workflow to the same page multiple times.")
   143	            )

   146	class WorkflowContentTypeForm(forms.Form):

   151	    class CheckboxSelectMultiple(forms.CheckboxSelectMultiple):
   152	        """Custom CheckboxSelectMultiple widget that renders errors for each content type ID"""
   153	
   154	        option_template_name = (
   155	            "wagtailadmin/workflows/includes/workflow_content_types_checkbox.html"
   156	        )
   157	
   158	        def get_errors_by_id(self, errors):
   159	            errors_by_id = {}
   160	            for error in errors.as_data():
   161	                ct_id = error.params and error.params.get("content_type_id")
   162	                errors_by_id.setdefault(ct_id, []).append(error)
   163	            return errors_by_id
   164	
   165	        def render_with_errors(
   166	            self, name, value, attrs=None, renderer=None, errors=None
   167	        ):
   168	            context = {
   169	                **self.get_context(name, value, attrs),
   170	                "errors_by_id": self.get_errors_by_id(errors),
   171	            }
   172	            return self._render(self.template_name, context, renderer)
   173	
   174	    content_types = ContentTypeMultipleChoiceField(
   175	        queryset=ContentType.objects.none(),
   176	        widget=CheckboxSelectMultiple(),
   177	        required=False,
   178	    )
   179	
   180	    def __init__(self, *args, workflow=None, **kwargs):
   181	        self.workflow = workflow
   182	        if workflow and workflow.pk and "initial" not in kwargs:
   183	            kwargs["initial"] = {"content_types": workflow.workflow_content_types.all()}
   184	
   185	        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
   186	
   187	        # Start with an always-false query, as Django can optimise it by
   188	        # returning an empty queryset without running any database queries.
   189	        workflow_enabled_q = Q(pk__in=[])
   190	
   191	        # Then union the query for each workflow-enabled model.
   192	        for model in get_workflow_enabled_models():
   193	            workflow_enabled_q |= Q(
   194	                app_label=model._meta.app_label, model=model._meta.model_name
   195	            )
   196	
   197	        self.fields["content_types"].queryset = ContentType.objects.filter(
   198	            workflow_enabled_q
   199	        )
   200	
   201	    def clean(self):
   202	        content_types = self.cleaned_data.get("content_types")
   203	        if not content_types:
   204	            return
   205	
   206	        existing_assignments = WorkflowContentType.objects.filter(
   207	            content_type__in=content_types,
   208	            workflow__active=True,
   209	        )
   210	        if self.workflow.pk:
   211	            existing_assignments = existing_assignments.exclude(workflow=self.workflow)
   212	
   213	        for assignment in existing_assignments:
   214	            self.add_error(
   215	                "content_types",
   216	                ValidationError(
   217	                    _(
   218	                        "Snippet '%(content_type)s' already has workflow '%(workflow_name)s' assigned."
   219	                    )
   220	                    % {
   221	                        "content_type": capfirst(assignment.content_type.name),
   222	                        "workflow_name": assignment.workflow,
   223	                    },
   224	                    code="existing_workflow_content_type",
   225	                    params={"content_type_id": assignment.content_type_id},
   226	                ),
   227	            )
   228	
   229	    def save(self, commit=True):
   230	        if not commit:
   231	            return
   232	
   233	        content_types = self.cleaned_data["content_types"]
   234	
   235	        with transaction.atomic():
   236	            # Remove any content types that are no longer selected
   237	            WorkflowContentType.objects.filter(workflow=self.workflow).exclude(
   238	                content_type__in=content_types
   239	            ).delete()
   240	
   241	            # Add any new content types, ignoring conflicts with existing ones
   242	            # to avoid additional query for existing content types
   243	            objects = [
   244	                WorkflowContentType(workflow=self.workflow, content_type=ct)
   245	                for ct in content_types
   246	            ]
   247	            WorkflowContentType.objects.bulk_create(objects, ignore_conflicts=True)
   248

   512	def remove_workflow(request, page_pk, workflow_pk=None):
   513	    # Remove a workflow from a page (specifically a single workflow if workflow_pk is set)
   514	    # Get the page
   515	    page = get_object_or_404(Page, id=page_pk)
   516	
   517	    # Check permissions
   518	    if not workflow_permission_policy.user_has_permission(request.user, "change"):
   519	        raise PermissionDenied
   520	
   521	    if hasattr(page, "workflowpage"):
   522	        # If workflow_pk is set, this will only remove the workflow if it its pk matches - this prevents accidental
   523	        # removal of the wrong workflow via a workflow edit page if the page listing is out of date
   524	        if not workflow_pk or workflow_pk == page.workflowpage.workflow.pk:
   525	            page.workflowpage.delete()
   526	            messages.success(
   527	                request,

   161	class ChildOfFilter(BaseFilterBackend):
   162	    """
   163	    Implements the ?child_of filter used to filter the results to only contain
   164	    pages that are direct children of the specified page.
   165	    """
   166	
   167	    def filter_queryset(self, request, queryset, view):
   168	        if "child_of" in request.GET:
   169	            try:
   170	                parent_page_id = int(request.GET["child_of"])
   171	                if parent_page_id < 0:
   172	                    raise ValueError()
   173	
   174	                parent_page = view.get_base_queryset().get(id=parent_page_id)

   107	class RelatedField(relations.RelatedField):
   108	    """
   109	    Serializes related objects (eg, foreign keys).
   110	
   111	    Example:
   112	
   113	    "feed_image": {
   114	        "id": 1,
   115	        "meta": {
   116	            "type": "wagtailimages.Image",
   117	            "detail_url": "http://api.example.com/v1/images/1/"
   118	        }
   119	    }
   120	    """
   121	
   122	    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
   123	        self.serializer_class = kwargs.pop("serializer_class")
   124	        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
   125	
   126	    def to_representation(self, value):
   127	        serializer = self.serializer_class(context=self.context)
   128	        return serializer.to_representation(value)

    17	class BatchRedirectCreator(BatchCreator):
    18	    """
    19	    A specialized ``BatchCreator`` class for saving ``Redirect`` objects.
    20	    """
    21	
    22	    model = Redirect
    23	
    24	    def pre_process(self):
    25	        # delete any existing automatically-created redirects that might clash
    26	        # with the items in `self.items`
    27	        clashes_q = Q()
    28	        for item in self.items:
    29	            clashes_q |= Q(old_path=item.old_path, site_id=item.site_id)
    30	        Redirect.objects.filter(automatically_created=True).filter(clashes_q).delete()
    31	
    32	    def post_process(self):
    33	        if not apps.is_installed("wagtail.contrib.frontend_cache"):
    34	            return
    35	
    36	        batch = PurgeBatch()
    37	
    38	        site_root_paths = Site.get_site_root_paths()
    39	
    40	        for redirect in self.items:
    41	            batch.add_urls(redirect.old_links(site_root_paths))
    42	
    43	        batch.purge()

    46	def autocreate_redirects_on_slug_change(

    54	
    55	    # Determine sites to create redirects for
    56	    sites = Site.objects.filter(

    58	            option.site_id
    59	            for option in instance._get_relevant_site_root_paths(cache_object=instance)
    60	        ]

    66	def autocreate_redirects_on_page_move(
    67	    instance: Page,
    68	    url_path_after: str,
    69	    url_path_before: str,
    70	    **kwargs,
    71	) -> None:
    72	    if not getattr(settings, "WAGTAILREDIRECTS_AUTO_CREATE", True):
    73	        return None
    74	
    75	    if url_path_after == url_path_before:
    76	        # Redirects are not needed for a page 'reorder'
    77	        return None
    78	
    79	    # NB: create_redirects expects specific instances
    80	    instance = instance.specific
    81	
    82	    # Simulate an 'old_page' by copying the specific instance and resetting
    83	    # the in-memory `url_path` value to what it was before the move
    84	    page_old = type(instance)()
    85	    page_old.__dict__.update(instance.__dict__)
    86	    page_old.url_path = url_path_before
    87	
    88	    # This value is used to prevent creation redirects that link
    89	    # from one site to another
    90	    new_site_ids = {
    91	        item.site_id
    92	        for item in instance._get_relevant_site_root_paths(cache_object=instance)
    93	    }
    94	
    95	    # Determine sites to create redirects for
    96	    sites = Site.objects.exclude(root_page=instance).filter(
    97	        id__in=[
    98	            item.site_id
    99	            for item in page_old._get_relevant_site_root_paths(cache_object=instance)
   100	            if item.site_id in new_site_ids
   101	        ]
   102	    )
   103	    create_redirects(page=instance, page_old=page_old, sites=sites)

   106	def _page_urls_for_sites(
   107	    page: Page, sites: tuple[Site], cache_target: Page
   108	) -> set[tuple[Site, str, str]]:
   109	    urls = set()
   110	    for site in sites:
   111	        # use a `HttpRequest` to influence the return value
   112	        request = get_dummy_request(site=site)
   113	        # reuse cached site root paths if available
   114	        if hasattr(cache_target, "_wagtail_cached_site_root_paths"):
   115	            request._wagtail_cached_site_root_paths = (
   116	                cache_target._wagtail_cached_site_root_paths
   117	            )
   118	
   119	        url_parts = page.get_url_parts(request)
   120	        if url_parts is None:
   121	            continue
   122	        site_id, root_url, page_path = url_parts
   123	
   124	        if page_path:
   125	            for route_path in page.get_route_paths():
   126	                normalized_route_path = Redirect.normalise_page_route_path(route_path)
   127	                old_path = Redirect.normalise_path(
   128	                    page_path.rstrip("/") + (normalized_route_path or "/")
   129	                )
   130	                urls.add((site, old_path, normalized_route_path))
   131	
   132	        # copy cached site root paths to `cache_target` to retain benefits
   133	        cache_target._wagtail_cached_site_root_paths = (
   134	            request._wagtail_cached_site_root_paths
   135	        )
   136	
   137	    return urls

   140	def create_redirects(page: Page, page_old: Page, sites: Iterable[Site]) -> None:
   141	    url_path_length = len(page.url_path)
   142	    sites = tuple(sites)
   143	
   144	    if not sites:
   145	        return None
   146	
   147	    logger.info(f"Creating redirects for page: '{page}' id={page.id}")
   148	
   149	    # For bulk-creating redirects in batches
   150	    batch = BatchRedirectCreator(max_size=2000, ignore_conflicts=True)
   151	
   152	    # Treat the page that was updated / moved separately to it's descendants,
   153	    # because there may be changes to fields other than `slug` or `url_path`
   154	    # that impact the URL.
   155	    old_urls = _page_urls_for_sites(page_old, sites, cache_target=page)
   156	    new_urls = _page_urls_for_sites(page, sites, cache_target=page)
   157	
   158	    # Add redirects for urls that have changed
   159	    changed_urls = old_urls - new_urls
   160	    for site, old_path, route_path in changed_urls:
   161	        batch.add(
   162	            old_path=old_path,
   163	            site=site,
   164	            redirect_page=page,
   165	            redirect_page_route_path=route_path,

   171	    # change, so we can use in-memory manipulation of `url_path` to figure out what
   172	    # the old URLS were

   182	        old_urls = _page_urls_for_sites(descendant, sites, cache_target=page)

   189	                site=site,
   190	                redirect_page=descendant,
   191	                redirect_page_route_path=route_path,
   192	                automatically_created=True,
   193	            )
   194	
   195	    # Process the final batch
   196	    batch.process()

    15	def register_signal_handlers():
    16	    # Get list of models that are page types
    17	    Page = apps.get_model("wagtailcore", "Page")
    18	    indexed_models = [model for model in apps.get_models() if issubclass(model, Page)]

    21	    for model in indexed_models:

    20	    Outputs a page's URL as relative (/foo/bar/) if it's within the same site as the
    21	    current page, or absolute (http://example.com/foo/bar/) if not.
    22	    If kwargs contains a fallback view name and page is None, the fallback view url will be returned.
    23	    """
    24	    if page is None and fallback:
    25	        return resolve_url(fallback)
    26	
    27	    if not isinstance(page, Page):
    28	        raise ValueError("pageurl tag expected a Page object, got %r" % page)
    29	
    30	    return page.get_url(request=context.get("request"))

    35	    """
    36	    Outputs a page's absolute URL (http://example.com/foo/bar/)
    37	    If kwargs contains a fallback view name and page is None, the fallback view url will be returned.
    38	    """
    39	    if page is None and fallback:
    40	        fallback_url = resolve_url(fallback)
    41	        if fallback_url and "request" in context and fallback_url[0] == "/":
    42	            fallback_url = context["request"].build_absolute_uri(fallback_url)
    43	        return fallback_url

    53	    """
    54	    Returns the URL for the page that has the given slug.
    55	
    56	    First tries to find a page on the current site. If that fails or a request
    57	    is not available in the context, then returns the URL for the first page
    58	    that matches the slug on any site.
    59	    """
    60	
    61	    page = None
    62	    try:
    63	        site = Site.find_for_request(context["request"])
    64	        current_site = site
    65	    except KeyError:
    66	        # No site object found - allow the fallback below to take place.
    67	        pass
    68	    else:
    69	        if current_site is not None:
    70	            page = Page.objects.in_site(current_site).filter(slug=slug).first()
    71	
    72	    # If no page is found, fall back to searching the whole tree.
    73	    if page is None:
    74	        page = Page.objects.filter(slug=slug).first()
    75	
    76	    if page:
    77	        # call pageurl() instead of page.relative_url() here so we get the ``accepts_kwarg`` logic
    78	        return pageurl(context, page)

   363	    def render_email(self, form):
   364	        content = []
   365	
   366	        cleaned_data = form.cleaned_data
   367	        for field in form:
   368	            if field.name not in cleaned_data:
   369	                continue
   370	
   371	            value = cleaned_data.get(field.name)
   372	
   373	            if isinstance(value, list):
   374	                value = ", ".join(value)
   375	
   376	            # Format dates and datetime(s) with SHORT_DATE(TIME)_FORMAT
   377	            if isinstance(value, datetime.datetime):
   378	                value = date_format(value, "SHORT_DATETIME_FORMAT")
   379	            elif isinstance(value, datetime.date):
   380	                value = date_format(value, "SHORT_DATE_FORMAT")
   381	
   382	            content.append(f"{field.label}: {value}")
   383	
   384	        return "\n".join(content)

    44	        # consider an invalid field as empty. For example, leaving the
    45	        # page field empty and entering an invalid external_link_url
    46	        # shouldn't raise an error about needing to enter a page or URL,
    47	        # since the user *has* entered (or tried to enter) a URL.
    48	        page = self["page"].value()
    49	        external_link_url = self["external_link_url"].value()
    50	        external_link_text = self["external_link_text"].value()
    51	
    52	        # Must supply a page or external_link_url (but not both)
    53	        if page is None:

    18	class ChooseParentView(LocaleMixin, WagtailAdminTemplateMixin, FormView):
    19	    template_name = "wagtailadmin/pages/choose_parent.html"
    20	    model = Page
    21	    index_url_name = None
    22	    page_title = gettext_lazy("Choose parent")
    23	
    24	    def get_valid_parent_pages(self, user):
    25	        """
    26	        Identifies possible parent pages for the current user by first looking
    27	        at allowed_parent_page_models() on self.model to limit options to the
    28	        correct type of page, then checking permissions on those individual
    29	        pages to make sure we have permission to add a subpage to it.
    30	        """
    31	        # Get queryset of pages where this page type can be added
    32	        allowed_parent_page_content_types = list(
    33	            ContentType.objects.get_for_models(
    34	                *self.model.allowed_parent_page_models()
    35	            ).values()
    36	        )
    37	        allowed_parent_pages = Page.objects.filter(
    38	            content_type__in=allowed_parent_page_content_types
    39	        )
    40	
    41	        # Filter by locale if i18n is enabled
    42	        if self.locale:
    43	            allowed_parent_pages = allowed_parent_pages.filter(locale=self.locale)
    44	
    45	        # Get queryset of pages where the user has permission to add subpages
    46	        if user.is_superuser:
    47	            pages_where_user_can_add = Page.objects.all()
    48	        else:
    49	            pages_where_user_can_add = Page.objects.none()
    50	
    51	            perms = {
    52	                perm
    53	                for perm in page_permission_policy.get_cached_permissions_for_user(user)
    54	                if perm.permission.codename == "add_page"
    55	            }
    56	
    57	            for perm in perms:
    58	                # user has add permission on any subpage of perm.page
    59	                # (including perm.page itself)
    60	                pages_where_user_can_add |= Page.objects.descendant_of(
    61	                    perm.page, inclusive=True
    62	                )
    63	
    64	        # Combine them
    65	        return allowed_parent_pages & pages_where_user_can_add
    66	
    67	    def get(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
    68	        parents = self.get_valid_parent_pages(request.user)
    69	
    70	        # Only fetch the IDs for the first two parents to check if there's only
    71	        # one valid parent, and if so, redirect to the add page view with that
    72	        # parent pre-selected
    73	        parent_ids = parents.values_list("pk", flat=True)[:2]
    74	        if len(parent_ids) == 1:
    75	            parent_id = quote(parent_ids[0])
    76	            model_opts = self.model._meta
    77	            return redirect(
    78	                "wagtailadmin_pages:add",
    79	                model_opts.app_label,
    80	                model_opts.model_name,
    81	                parent_id,
    82	            )
    83	
    84	        # The page can be added in multiple places, so proceed with rendering
    85	        # the view's form so that the parent can be specified
    86	        return super().get(request, *args, **kwargs)
    87	
    88	    def get_form(self):
    89	        if self.request.method == "POST":
    90	            return ParentChooserForm(self.model, self.request.user, self.request.POST)
    91	        return ParentChooserForm(self.model, self.request.user)

  1222	    def get_default_workflow(cls):
  1223	        """
  1224	        Returns the active workflow assigned to the model.
  1225	
  1226	        For non-``Page`` models, workflows are assigned to the model's content type,
  1227	        thus shared across all instances instead of being assigned to individual
  1228	        instances (unless :meth:`~WorkflowMixin.get_workflow` is overridden).
  1229	
  1230	        This method is used to determine the workflow to use when creating new
  1231	        instances of the model. On ``Page`` models, this method is unused as the
  1232	        workflow can be determined from the parent page's workflow.
  1233	        """
  1234	        if not getattr(settings, "WAGTAIL_WORKFLOW_ENABLED", True):
  1235	            return None

  1237	        content_type = ContentType.objects.get_for_model(cls, for_concrete_model=False)

  1240	                workflow__active=True,
   115	            # implicit type casts when querying on GenericRelations. We also need
   116	            # to cast the object_id to integer when querying the pages for the same reason.
   117	            # https://code.djangoproject.com/ticket/16055

   119	            # filter can be changed to:

   121	            pages_owned_by_user = Q(
   122	                base_content_type_id=get_default_page_content_type().id
   123	            ) & Exists(
   124	                Page.objects.filter(
   125	                    owner=request.user,
   126	                    id=Cast(OuterRef("object_id"), output_field=IntegerField()),
   127	                )
   128	            )
   129	            # Find in progress workflow states which are either requested by the user or on pages owned by the user
   130	            context["workflow_states"] = (
   131	                WorkflowState.objects.active()
   132	                .filter(pages_owned_by_user | Q(requested_by=request.user))
   133	                .prefetch_related(
   134	                    "content_object",
   135	                    "content_object__latest_revision",
   136	                )
   137	                .select_related(
   138	                    "current_task_state",
   139	                    "current_task_state__task",
   140	                )
   141	                .order_by("-current_task_state__started_at")
   142	            )
   143	            # Filter out workflow states where the GenericForeignKey points to
   144	            # a nonexistent object. This can happen if the model does not define
   145	            # a GenericRelation to WorkflowState and the instance is deleted.
   146	            context["workflow_states"] = [
   147	                state for state in context["workflow_states"] if state.content_object
   148	            ]
   149	        else:
   150	            context["workflow_states"] = WorkflowState.objects.none()
   151	        context["request"] = request
   152	        return context
